1528                  if ("never".equalsIgnoreCase(v) || "false".equalsIgnoreCase(v))
1529                     return new DebugResult(false, format, level, false);
1530                  if ("conditional".equalsIgnoreCase(v))
1531                     return new DebugResult("true".equalsIgnoreCase(req.getHeader("Debug")), format, level, true);
1532               }
1533            }
1534            return resolve(context.getContext(), req);
1535         }
1536      };
1537   });
1538
1539   /**
1540    * The default request attributes contributed by {@code @Rest(defaultRequestAttributes)} for this resource.
1541    *
1542    * <p>
1543    * Walks {@code @Rest} annotations parent-to-child, resolving each attribute string and parsing it as a
1544    * key=value or key:value pair. A named bean-store override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES
1545    * the accumulated result.
1546    */
1547   private final Memoizer<NamedAttributeMap> defaultRequestAttributes = memoizer(() -> {
1548      var v = Holder.of(NamedAttributeMap.create());
1549      // Resolution moved onto the unified noInherit-aware walk (cleanup #3): getRestAnnotationsForProperty yields
1550      // the same parent-to-child order getRestAnnotationsTopDown did for non-mixin contexts, plus host→mixin
1551      // inheritance, noInherit cutoff, and @Mixin override injection for mixin sub-contexts.
1552      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_defaultRequestAttributes).forEach(ai -> Arrays.stream(ai.inner().defaultRequestAttributes())
1553         .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
1554         .map(this::resolve)
1555         .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
1556         .map(BasicNamedAttribute::ofPair)
1557         .forEach(v.get()::add));
1558      beanStore().createBeanFromMethod(NamedAttributeMap.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, PROP_defaultRequestAttributes), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1559      return v.get();
1560   });
1561
1562   /**
1563    * The default request headers contributed by {@code @Rest(defaultRequestHeaders)},
1564    * {@code @Rest(defaultAccept)}, and {@code @Rest(defaultContentType)} for this resource.
1565    *
1566    * <p>
1567    * Walks {@code @Rest} annotations parent-to-child, resolving each header string. A named bean-store
1568    * override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the accumulated result.
1569    */
1570   private final Memoizer<HttpHeaderList> defaultRequestHeaders = memoizer(() -> {
1571      var v = Holder.of(HttpHeaderList.create());
1572      // Resolution moved onto the unified noInherit-aware walk (cleanup #3) — see defaultRequestAttributes.
1573      // defaultAccept/defaultContentType are folded into this same walk (as they always were); they key off
1574      // the defaultRequestHeaders property for noInherit/override purposes.
1575      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_defaultRequestHeaders).forEach(ai -> {
1576         Rest a = ai.inner();
1577         Arrays.stream(a.defaultRequestHeaders()).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).map(this::resolve).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).map(HttpStringHeader::ofPair).forEach(v.get()::setDefault);
1578         var defaultAccept = resolve(a.defaultAccept());
1579         if (isNotBlank(defaultAccept))
1580            v.get().setDefault(Accept.of(defaultAccept));
1581         var defaultContentType = resolve(a.defaultContentType());
1582         if (isNotBlank(defaultContentType))
1583            v.get().setDefault(ContentType.of(defaultContentType));
1584      });
1585      beanStore().createBeanFromMethod(HttpHeaderList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, PROP_defaultRequestHeaders), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1586      return v.get();
1587   });
1588
1589   /**
1590    * The default response headers contributed by {@code @Rest(defaultResponseHeaders)} for this resource.
1591    *
1592    * <p>
1593    * Walks {@code @Rest} annotations parent-to-child, resolving each header string. A named bean-store
1594    * override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the accumulated result.
1595    */
1596   private final Memoizer<HttpHeaderList> defaultResponseHeaders = memoizer(() -> {
1597      var v = Holder.of(HttpHeaderList.create());
1598      // Resolution moved onto the unified noInherit-aware walk (cleanup #3) — see defaultRequestAttributes.
1599      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_defaultResponseHeaders).forEach(ai -> Arrays.stream(ai.inner().defaultResponseHeaders()).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).map(this::resolve).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).map(HttpStringHeader::ofPair).forEach(v.get()::setDefault));
1600      beanStore().createBeanFromMethod(HttpHeaderList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, PROP_defaultResponseHeaders), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1601      return v.get();
1602   });
1603
1604   /**
1605    * Methods annotated with {@link RestDestroy @RestDestroy} and their invokers.
1606    */
1607   private final Memoizer<LifecycleInvokerPair> destroyInvokerPair = memoizer(() -> buildLifecycleInvokerPair(() -> {
1608      var bs = beanStore();
1609      var v = Holder.of(MethodList.of(getAnnotatedMethods(resource(), RestDestroy.class).toList()));
1610      bs.createBeanFromMethod(MethodList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, "destroyMethods"), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1611      return v.get();
1612   }));
1613
1614   /**
1615    * Fully-configured {@link EncoderSet.Builder} for this resource, populated from the
1616    * {@code @Rest(encoders)} annotation chain and any {@code @Bean} override.
1617    *
1618    * <p>
1619    * Starts with {@link IdentityEncoder} as the implicit default. A bean-store type override
1620    * or bean-store instance override REPLACES the builder or its impl. Annotation entries
1621    * (parent-to-child) are appended after the default. A {@code @Bean} factory method
1622    * REPLACES the impl with the returned value.
1623    */
1624   private final Memoizer<EncoderSet.Builder> encodersBuilder = memoizer(() -> {
1625      var bs = beanStore();
1626      var v = Holder.of(EncoderSet.create(bs));
1627      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_encoders).forEach(ai -> v.get().add(ai.inner().encoders()));
1628      bs.createBeanFromMethod(EncoderSet.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, v.get()).ifPresent(x -> v.get().impl(x));
1629      return v.get();
1630   });
1631
1632   /**
1633    * The {@link EncoderSet} for this resource, built from the self-contained encoder builder memoizer.
1634    */
1635   private final Memoizer<EncoderSet> encoders = memoizer(() -> encodersBuilder.get().build());
1636
1637   /**
1638    * Methods annotated with {@link RestEndCall @RestEndCall} and their invokers.
1639    */
1640   private final Memoizer<LifecycleInvokerPair> endCallInvokerPair = memoizer(() -> buildLifecycleInvokerPair(() -> {
1641      var bs = beanStore();
1642      var v = Holder.of(MethodList.of(getAnnotatedMethods(resource(), RestEndCall.class).toList()));
1643      bs.createBeanFromMethod(MethodList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, "endCallMethods"), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1644      return v.get();
1645   }));
1646
1647   /**
1648    * The {@link JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder} for this resource, with annotation work applied.
1649    */
1650   private final Memoizer<JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder> jsonSchemaGeneratorBuilder = memoizer(() -> {
1651      var bs = beanStore();
1652      var v = Holder.of(JsonSchemaGenerator.create());
1653      bs.createBeanFromMethod(JsonSchemaGenerator.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod).ifPresent(x -> v.get().impl(x));
1654      v.get().apply(annotationWork);
1655      return v.get();
1656   });
1657
1658   /**
1659    * The {@link JsonSchemaGenerator} for this resource.
1660    */
1661   private final Memoizer<JsonSchemaGenerator> jsonSchemaGenerator = memoizer(() -> jsonSchemaGeneratorBuilder.get().build());
1662
1663   /**
1664    * The {@link Logger} for this resource.
1665    *
1666    * <p>
1667    * Defaults to {@code Logger.getLogger(resourceClass.getName())}. A bean-store override or
1668    * {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the default.
1669    */
1670   private final Memoizer<Logger> logger = memoizer(() -> {
1671      var v = Holder.of(Logger.getLogger(cn(resourceClass())));
1672      beanStore().createBeanFromMethod(Logger.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1673      return v.get();
1674   });
1675
1676   /**
1677    * The {@link Messages} bundle for this resource.
1678    *
1679    * <p>
1680    * Walks {@code @Rest} annotations parent-to-child, resolving each {@code messages} location string
1681    * against the bootstrap resolver (full resolver not yet available — it depends on {@code getMessages()}).
1682    * A bean-store override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the result.
1683    */
1684   private final Memoizer<Messages> messages = memoizer(() -> {
1685      var b = Messages.create(resourceClass());
1686      // Walk @Rest annotations parent-to-child; child wins because location() prepends.
1687      // Resolve location strings against the simple resolver — full resolver isn't available yet
1688      // (it depends on getMessages()).
1689      // NOTE (cleanup #3): messages is structurally bundle-CHAINED (not list/replace-merged), so it keeps its
1690      // own local location walk + Messages.chain rather than routing through getRestAnnotationsForProperty
1691      // (which would double-count host locations for a mixin context).  The two @Mixin hooks are layered on
1692      // explicitly instead: (1) a host @Mixin(messages=...) location is applied last (highest priority), and
1693      // (2) the host→mixin chain cutoff consults the unified noInherit set, so @Mixin(noInherit="messages")
1694      // cuts the host bundle exactly like the mixin class's own @Rest(noInherit="messages").
1695      var vrs = getBootstrapVarResolver().createSession();
1696      getRestAnnotationsTopDown().forEach(ai -> ai.getString(PROPERTY_messages).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).ifPresent(s -> b.location(vrs.resolve(s))));
1697      var rmMessages = resolvedMixinField();
1698      if (rmMessages != null) {
1699         var mm = rmMessages.overrides().messages();
1700         if (StringUtils.isNotBlank(mm))
1701            b.location(vrs.resolve(mm));
1702      }
1703      var override = beanStore().createBeanFromMethod(Messages.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, b).orElse(null);
1704      var local = nn(override) ? override : b.build();
1705      var parent = parentContext();
1706      // Use the SVL-free isNoInheritLiteral (NOT the noInherit memoizer) to avoid a resolver cycle:
1707      // noInherit -> resolveCdl -> getVarResolver -> getMessages -> messages.  It now also consults the
1708      // host @Mixin(noInherit=...) tokens so a @Mixin can cut the host bundle just like the mixin class's own.
1709      if (isMixinContextField() && nn(parent) && !isNoInheritLiteral(PROPERTY_messages))
1710         return Messages.chain(local, parent.getMessages());
1711      return local;
1712   });
1713
1714   /**
1715    * The {@link MethodExecStore} for this resource, wired to the {@link ThrownStore}.
1716    *
1717    * <p>
1718    * A bean-store override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the result.
1719    */
1720   private final Memoizer<MethodExecStore> methodExecStore = memoizer(() -> {
1721      var bs = beanStore();
1722      var b = MethodExecStore.create(bs).thrownStoreOnce(getThrownStore());
1723      bs.createBeanFromMethod(MethodExecStore.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, b).ifPresent(b::impl);
1724      return b.build();
1725   });
1726
1727   /**
1728    * Fully-configured {@link ParserSet.Builder} for this resource, populated from the
1729    * {@code @Rest(parsers)} annotation chain and any {@code @Bean} override.
1730    *
1731    * <p>
1732    * Starts with an empty set. A bean-store type or instance override REPLACES the builder or its impl.
1733    * Annotation entries (parent-to-child) are appended. A {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the impl.
1734    */
1735   private final Memoizer<ParserSet.Builder> parsersBuilder = memoizer(() -> {
1736      var bs = beanStore();
1737      var v = Holder.of(ParserSet.create(bs));
1738      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_parsers).forEach(ai -> v.get().add(ai.inner().parsers()));
1739      bs.createBeanFromMethod(ParserSet.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, v.get()).ifPresent(x -> v.get().impl(x));
1740      return v.get();
1741   });
1742
1743   /**
1744    * The {@link ParserSet} for this resource, built from the self-contained parser builder memoizer.
1745    */
1746   private final Memoizer<ParserSet> parsers = memoizer(() -> parsersBuilder.get().build());
1747
1748   /**
1749    * The {@link HttpPartParser.Creator} for this resource, with annotation work applied.
1750    */
1751   private final Memoizer<HttpPartParser.Creator> partParserCreator = memoizer(() -> {
1752      var bs = beanStore();
1753      Holder<HttpPartParser.Creator> v = Holder.of(HttpPartParser.creator().type(OpenApiParser.class));
1754      o(resource().get() instanceof HttpPartParser x ? x : null).ifPresent(x -> v.get().impl(x));
1755      bs.createBeanFromMethod(HttpPartParser.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod).ifPresent(x -> v.get().impl(x));
1756      v.get().apply(annotationWork);
1757      return v.get();
1758   });
1759
1760   /**
1761    * The {@link HttpPartParser} for this resource.
1762    *
1763    * <p>
1764    * Starts from the creator (general config annotations already applied) and adds the
1765    * {@code @Rest(partParser)} type override from the most specific annotation in the hierarchy.
1766    */
1767   private final Memoizer<HttpPartParser> partParser = memoizer(() -> {
1768      var creator = partParserCreator.get();
1769      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_partParser)
1770         .map(ai -> ai.inner().partParser())
1771         .filter(ClassUtils::isNotVoid)
1772         .reduce((a, b) -> b)
1773         .ifPresent(creator::type);
1774      return creator.create();
1775   });
1776
1777   /**
1778    * The {@link HttpPartSerializer.Creator} for this resource, with annotation work applied.
1779    */
1780   private final Memoizer<HttpPartSerializer.Creator> partSerializerCreator = memoizer(() -> {
1781      var bs = beanStore();
1782      Holder<HttpPartSerializer.Creator> v = Holder.of(HttpPartSerializer.creator().type(OpenApiSerializer.class));
1783      o(resource().get() instanceof HttpPartSerializer x ? x : null).ifPresent(x -> v.get().impl(x));
1784      bs.createBeanFromMethod(HttpPartSerializer.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod).ifPresent(x -> v.get().impl(x));
1785      v.get().apply(annotationWork);
1786      return v.get();
1787   });
1788
1789   /**
1790    * The {@link HttpPartSerializer} for this resource.
1791    *
1792    * <p>
1793    * Starts from the creator (general config annotations already applied) and adds the
1794    * {@code @Rest(partSerializer)} type override from the most specific annotation in the hierarchy.
1795    */
1796   private final Memoizer<HttpPartSerializer> partSerializer = memoizer(() -> {
1797      var creator = partSerializerCreator.get();
1798      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_partSerializer)
1799         .map(ai -> ai.inner().partSerializer())
1800         .filter(ClassUtils::isNotVoid)
1801         .reduce((a, b) -> b)
1802         .ifPresent(creator::type);
1803      return creator.create();
1804   });
1805
1806   /**
1807    * Methods annotated with {@link RestPostCall @RestPostCall}.
1808    */
1809   private final Memoizer<MethodList> postCallMethods = memoizer(() -> {
1810      var bs = beanStore();
1811      var v = Holder.of(MethodList.of(getAnnotatedMethods(resource(), RestPostCall.class).toList()));
1812      bs.createBeanFromMethod(MethodList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, "postCallMethods"), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1813      return v.get();
1814   });
1815
1816   /**
1817    * Pre-resolved invokers for this context's local {@code @RestPostCall} methods, bound to this
1818    * context's resource instance and bean-store-resolved arg resolvers. Used by the {@link #postCall(RestOpSession)}
1819    * parent-chain walk so that host hooks fire before mixin hooks on mixin-endpoint requests.
1820    *
1821    * <p>
1822    * Hook methods aren't op-specific, so the wrapping bean store stubs in the op-only {@link UrlPathMatcher}
1823    * binding with {@code null} so that {@link PathArg#create PathArg.create()} (and
1824    * other op-aware resolvers) can be called by {@link #findRestOperationArgs(java.lang.reflect.Method, BeanStore)}
1825    * and abstain (return {@code null}) for parameters that aren't {@code @Path}-annotated. Using
1826    * {@code @Path} on a {@code @RestPostCall} method is not supported.
1827    */
1828   private final Memoizer<RestOpInvoker[]> localPostCallInvokers = memoizer(() -> {
1829      var bs = new BasicBeanStore(beanStore());
1830      var pm = getPathMatcher() != null ? getPathMatcher() : UrlPathMatcher.of("");
1831      bs.addBean(UrlPathMatcher.class, pm);
1832      bs.addBean(UrlPathMatcher[].class, new UrlPathMatcher[]{pm});
1833      return postCallMethods.get().stream()
1834         .map(x -> new RestOpInvoker(x, findRestOperationArgs(x, bs), getMethodExecStats(x), this::getResource))
1835         .toArray(RestOpInvoker[]::new);
1836   });
1837
1838   /**
1839    * Methods annotated with {@link RestPostInit @RestPostInit}{@code (childFirst=true)} and their invokers.
1840    */
1841   private final Memoizer<LifecycleInvokerPair> postInitChildFirstInvokerPair = memoizer(() -> buildLifecycleInvokerPair(() -> {
1842      var bs = beanStore();
1843      var v = Holder.of(MethodList.of(getAnnotatedMethods(resource(), RestPostInit.class)
1844         .filter(m -> rstream(AnnotationProvider.INSTANCE.find(RestPostInit.class, MethodInfo.of(m))).map(AnnotationInfo::inner).anyMatch(RestPostInit::childFirst))
1845         .toList()));
1846      bs.createBeanFromMethod(MethodList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, "postInitChildFirstMethods"), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1847      return v.get();
1848   }));
1849
1850   /**
1851    * Methods annotated with {@link RestPostInit @RestPostInit} and their invokers.
1852    */
1853   private final Memoizer<LifecycleInvokerPair> postInitInvokerPair = memoizer(() -> buildLifecycleInvokerPair(() -> {
1854      var bs = beanStore();
1855      var v = Holder.of(MethodList.of(getAnnotatedMethods(resource(), RestPostInit.class)
1856         .filter(m -> rstream(AnnotationProvider.INSTANCE.find(RestPostInit.class, MethodInfo.of(m))).map(AnnotationInfo::inner).anyMatch(x -> !x.childFirst()))
1857         .toList()));
1858      bs.createBeanFromMethod(MethodList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, "postInitMethods"), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1859      return v.get();
1860   }));
1861
1862   /**
1863    * Methods annotated with {@link RestPreCall @RestPreCall}.
1864    */
1865   private final Memoizer<MethodList> preCallMethods = memoizer(() -> {
1866      var bs = beanStore();
1867      var v = Holder.of(MethodList.of(getAnnotatedMethods(resource(), RestPreCall.class).toList()));
1868      bs.createBeanFromMethod(MethodList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, "preCallMethods"), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
1869      return v.get();
1870   });
1871
1872   /**
1873    * Pre-resolved invokers for this context's local {@code @RestPreCall} methods, bound to this
1874    * context's resource instance and bean-store-resolved arg resolvers. Used by the {@link #preCall(RestOpSession)}
1875    * parent-chain walk so that host hooks fire before mixin hooks on mixin-endpoint requests.
1876    *
1877    * <p>
1878    * Hook methods aren't op-specific, so the wrapping bean store stubs in the op-only {@link UrlPathMatcher}
1879    * binding with {@code null} so that {@link PathArg#create PathArg.create()} (and
1880    * other op-aware resolvers) can be called by {@link #findRestOperationArgs(java.lang.reflect.Method, BeanStore)}
1881    * and abstain (return {@code null}) for parameters that aren't {@code @Path}-annotated. Using
1882    * {@code @Path} on a {@code @RestPreCall} method is not supported.
1883    */
1884   private final Memoizer<RestOpInvoker[]> localPreCallInvokers = memoizer(() -> {
1885      var bs = new BasicBeanStore(beanStore());
1886      var pm = getPathMatcher() != null ? getPathMatcher() : UrlPathMatcher.of("");
1887      bs.addBean(UrlPathMatcher.class, pm);
1888      bs.addBean(UrlPathMatcher[].class, new UrlPathMatcher[]{pm});
1889      return preCallMethods.get().stream()
1890         .map(x -> new RestOpInvoker(x, findRestOperationArgs(x, bs), getMethodExecStats(x), this::getResource))
1891         .toArray(RestOpInvoker[]::new);
1892   });
1893
1894   /**
1895    * The supported response content types ({@code Accept} media types) for this resource.
1896    *
1897    * <p>
1898    * Walks {@code @Rest(produces)} annotations parent-to-child and returns the union. When no annotation
1899    * declares types, falls back to the intersection of all op-level serializer supported media types.
1900    */
1901   private final Memoizer<List<MediaType>> produces = memoizer(() -> {
1902      // Walk @Rest(produces=...) chain (parent-to-child); concat all entries in chain order.
1903      var fromAnnotations = new ArrayList<MediaType>();
1904      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_produces)
1905         .forEach(ai -> stream(ai.inner().produces()).map(MediaType::of).forEach(fromAnnotations::add));
1906      if (! fromAnnotations.isEmpty())
1907         return u(fromAnnotations);
1908      // Fall back to the intersection of opContexts' serializer-supported media types — matches legacy behavior.
1909      var opContexts = restOperations().getOpContexts();
1910      Set<MediaType> s = opContexts.isEmpty() ? emptySet() : toSet(opContexts.get(0).getSerializers().getSupportedMediaTypes());
1911      opContexts.forEach(x -> s.retainAll(x.getSerializers().getSupportedMediaTypes()));
1912      return u(toList(s));
1913   });
1914
1915   /**
1916    * JVM-wide cache of {@link ResponseProcessor} provider classes discovered via {@link ServiceLoader}.
1917    *
1918    * <p>
1919    * Populated lazily on first use and shared across all {@link RestContext} instances. Empty on a bare
1920    * {@code juneau-rest-server} classpath; non-empty only when an opt-in module (e.g.
1921    * {@code juneau-rest-server-reactive}) ships a
1922    * {@code META-INF/services/ResponseProcessor} provider file.
1923    */
1924   @SuppressWarnings({
1925      "java:S3077" // volatile is required for correct double-checked-locking safe-publication of this JVM-wide cache; the reference is publish-once and never compound-mutated.
1926   })
1927   private static volatile List<Class<? extends ResponseProcessor>> serviceLoaderResponseProcessors;
1928
1929   /**
1930    * Returns the module-contributed {@link ResponseProcessor} classes discovered via {@link ServiceLoader},
1931    * caching the result for the lifetime of the JVM.
1932    *
1933    * <p>
1934    * Provider <em>types</em> are resolved (not instantiated) so each {@link RestContext} can instantiate its
1935    * own bean-store-injected copy. Discovery failures are swallowed at {@code FINE} so a malformed provider
1936    * never fails resource startup; on a bare classpath the returned list is empty and the default processor
1937    * chain is unchanged.
1938    *
1939    * @return The discovered processor classes (possibly empty, never {@code null}).
1940    */
1941   private static List<Class<? extends ResponseProcessor>> discoverServiceLoaderResponseProcessors() {
1942      var p = serviceLoaderResponseProcessors;
1943      if (p == null) {
1944         synchronized (RestContext.class) {
1945            p = serviceLoaderResponseProcessors;
1946            if (p == null) {
1947               p = loadServiceLoaderResponseProcessors();
1948               serviceLoaderResponseProcessors = p;
1949            }
1950         }
1951      }
1952      return p;
1953   }
1954
1955   private static List<Class<? extends ResponseProcessor>> loadServiceLoaderResponseProcessors() {
1956      var out = new ArrayList<Class<? extends ResponseProcessor>>();
1957      try {
1958         for (var it = ServiceLoader.load(ResponseProcessor.class).stream().iterator(); it.hasNext();)
1959            addNextServiceLoaderResponseProcessor(out, it);
1960      } catch (ServiceConfigurationError | RuntimeException e) {
1961         LOG.log(Level.FINE, e, () -> "ServiceLoader for ResponseProcessor failed: " + e.getMessage());
1962      }
1963      return List.copyOf(out);
1964   }
1965
1966   private static void addNextServiceLoaderResponseProcessor(List<Class<? extends ResponseProcessor>> out, Iterator<ServiceLoader.Provider<ResponseProcessor>> it) {
1967      try {
1968         out.add(it.next().type());
1969      } catch (ServiceConfigurationError | RuntimeException e) {
1970         LOG.log(Level.FINE, e, () -> "Skipping ServiceLoader-discovered ResponseProcessor: " + e.getMessage());
1971      }
1972   }
1973
1974   /**
1975    * The ordered array of {@link ResponseProcessor} instances for this resource.
1976    *
1977    * <p>
1978    * Walks {@code @Rest(responseProcessors)} annotations parent-to-child (append order). A bean-store
1979    * override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the entire list.
1980    */
1981   private final Memoizer<ResponseProcessor[]> responseProcessors = memoizer(() -> {
1982      // Walk @Rest(responseProcessors=...) chain (parent-to-child via getRestAnnotationsForProperty).
1983      // DefaultConfig contributes the framework defaults at the top of the chain; resource-class entries append.
1984      // ResponseProcessorList.Builder.add(...) uses addAll (append) — final order: [DefaultConfig, parent, child].
1985      var bs = beanStore();
1986      var b = ResponseProcessorList.create(bs);
1987      // When enabled (default on-when-tracer), front-load the W3C trace-context processor so it
1988      // writes traceparent/tracestate headers before the body-rendering processors run. It short-circuits
1989      // at request time when no TracerHook stashed a trace context, so it's zero-cost on the no-tracer path.
1990      if (defaultResponseTraceparent)
1991         b.add(TraceContextResponseProcessor.class);
1992      // Front-load any module-contributed ResponseProcessors discovered via
1993      // ServiceLoader (e.g. the opt-in juneau-rest-server-reactive module's ReactiveResponseProcessor,
1994      // which must run ahead of AsyncResponseProcessor). Inert on a bare juneau-rest-server classpath —
1995      // when no module ships a META-INF/services/...ResponseProcessor provider file the list is empty and
1996      // the chain is identical to the pre-feature default.
1997      discoverServiceLoaderResponseProcessors().forEach(b::add);
1998      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_responseProcessors)
1999         .forEach(ai -> b.add(ai.inner().responseProcessors()));
2000      // @Bean method override REPLACES the entire annotation-derived list.
2001      var override = bs.createBeanFromMethod(ResponseProcessorList.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, b).orElse(null);
2002      return (nn(override) ? override : b.build()).toArray();
2003   });
2004
2005   /**
2006    * The ordered array of {@link RestOpArg} argument-resolver types for this resource.
2007    *
2008    * <p>
2009    * Walks {@code @Rest(restOpArgs)} annotations parent-to-child (prepend order, so child entries
2010    * take priority). A bean-store override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the entire list.
2011    */
2012   private final Memoizer<Class<? extends RestOpArg>[]> restOpArgs = memoizer(() -> {
2013      // Walk @Rest(restOpArgs=...) chain (parent-to-child via getRestAnnotationsForProperty).
2014      // DefaultConfig contributes the framework arg-resolver list; resource-class entries override.
2015      // RestOpArgList.Builder.add(...) uses prependAll — applying per-annotation in chain order yields
2016      // final order: [child, parent, DefaultConfig], matching the legacy apply-pass behavior.
2017      var bs = beanStore();
2018      var b = RestOpArgList.create(bs);
2019      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_restOpArgs)
2020         .forEach(ai -> b.add(ai.inner().restOpArgs()));
2021      // @Bean method override REPLACES the entire annotation-derived list.
2022      var override = bs.createBeanFromMethod(RestOpArgList.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, b).orElse(null);
2023      return (nn(override) ? override : b.build()).asArray();
2024   });
2025
2026   /**
2027    * Fully-configured {@link SerializerSet.Builder} for this resource, populated from the
2028    * {@code @Rest(serializers)} annotation chain and any {@code @Bean} override.
2029    *
2030    * <p>
2031    * Starts with an empty set. A bean-store type or instance override REPLACES the builder or its impl.
2032    * Annotation entries (parent-to-child) are appended. A {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the impl.
2033    */
2034   private final Memoizer<SerializerSet.Builder> serializersBuilder = memoizer(() -> {
2035      var bs = beanStore();
2036      var v = Holder.of(SerializerSet.create(bs));
2037      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_serializers).forEach(ai -> v.get().add(ai.inner().serializers()));
2038      bs.createBeanFromMethod(SerializerSet.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, v.get()).ifPresent(x -> v.get().impl(x));
2039      return v.get();
2040   });
2041
2042   /**
2043    * The {@link SerializerSet} for this resource, built from the self-contained serializer builder memoizer.
2044    */
2045   private final Memoizer<SerializerSet> serializers = memoizer(() -> serializersBuilder.get().build());
2046
2047   /**
2048    * Methods annotated with {@link RestStartCall @RestStartCall} and their invokers.
2049    */
2050   private final Memoizer<LifecycleInvokerPair> startCallInvokerPair = memoizer(() -> buildLifecycleInvokerPair(() -> {
2051      var bs = beanStore();
2052      var v = Holder.of(MethodList.of(getAnnotatedMethods(resource(), RestStartCall.class).toList()));
2053      bs.createBeanFromMethod(MethodList.class, resource().get(), x -> isBeanMethod(x, "startCallMethods"), v.get()).ifPresent(v::set);
2054      return v.get();
2055   }));
2056
2057   /**
2058    * The {@link StaticFiles} provider for this resource.
2059    *
2060    * <p>
2061    * Defaults to {@link BasicStaticFiles}. {@code @Rest(staticFiles=X)} most-derived non-{@code Void} class wins.
2062    * A bean-store override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the result.
2063    */
2064   private final Memoizer<StaticFiles> staticFiles = memoizer(() -> {
2065      var bs = beanStore();
2066      var creator = BeanInstantiator.of(StaticFiles.class, bs).type(BasicStaticFiles.class).noBuilder();
2067      bs.getBeanType(StaticFiles.class).ifPresent(creator::type);
2068      // @Rest(staticFiles=X) — most-derived non-Void wins. See callLogger for the reduce-last rationale.
2069      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_staticFiles)
2070         .map(ai -> ai.inner().staticFiles())
2071         .filter(c -> c != StaticFiles.Void.class)
2072         .reduce((first, second) -> second)
2073         .ifPresent(creator::type);
2074      bs.createBeanFromMethod(StaticFiles.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod).ifPresent(creator::impl);
2075      return creator.asOptional().orElse(null);
2076   });
2077
2078   /**
2079    * The {@link SwaggerProvider} for this resource.
2080    *
2081    * <p>
2082    * Defaults to {@link BasicSwaggerProvider}. {@code @Rest(swaggerProvider=X)} most-derived
2083    * non-{@code Void} class wins. A bean-store override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the result.
2084    */
2085   private final Memoizer<SwaggerProvider> swaggerProvider = memoizer(() -> {
2086      var bs = beanStore();
2087      // Register the resource class as a SwaggerResource bean so SwaggerProvider implementations
2088      // can resolve it via constructor injection (rather than reading it off a Builder).
2089      bs.addBean(SwaggerResource.class, SwaggerResource.of(resourceClass()));
2090      var creator = BeanInstantiator.of(SwaggerProvider.class, bs).type(BasicSwaggerProvider.class).noBuilder();
2091      bs.getBeanType(SwaggerProvider.class).ifPresent(creator::type);
2092      // @Rest(swaggerProvider=X) — most-derived non-Void wins. See callLogger for the reduce-last rationale.
2093      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_swaggerProvider)
2094         .map(ai -> ai.inner().swaggerProvider())
2095         .filter(c -> c != SwaggerProvider.Void.class)
2096         .reduce((first, second) -> second)
2097         .ifPresent(creator::type);
2098      bs.createBeanFromMethod(SwaggerProvider.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod).ifPresent(creator::impl);
2099      return creator.asOptional().orElse(null);
2100   });
2101
2102   /**
2103    * The {@link OpenApiProvider} for this resource.
2104    *
2105    * <p>
2106    * Defaults to {@link BasicOpenApiProvider}. {@code @Rest(openApiProvider=X)} most-derived
2107    * non-{@code Void} class wins. A bean-store override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the result.
2108    */
2109   private final Memoizer<OpenApiProvider> openApiProvider = memoizer(() -> {
2110      var bs = beanStore();
2111      bs.addBean(OpenApiResource.class, OpenApiResource.of(resourceClass()));
2112      var creator = BeanInstantiator.of(OpenApiProvider.class, bs).type(BasicOpenApiProvider.class).noBuilder();
2113      bs.getBeanType(OpenApiProvider.class).ifPresent(creator::type);
2114      // @Rest(openApiProvider=X) — most-derived non-Void wins.
2115      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_openApiProvider)
2116         .map(ai -> ai.inner().openApiProvider())
2117         .filter(c -> c != OpenApiProvider.Void.class)
2118         .reduce((first, second) -> second)
2119         .ifPresent(creator::type);
2120      bs.createBeanFromMethod(OpenApiProvider.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod).ifPresent(creator::impl);
2121      return creator.asOptional().orElse(null);
2122   });
2123
2124   /**
2125    * The {@link ThrownStore} for this resource, used to track exception statistics.
2126    *
2127    * <p>
2128    * Inherits from the parent context's store when one is present. A bean-store override or
2129    * {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the result.
2130    */
2131   private final Memoizer<ThrownStore> thrownStore = memoizer(() -> {
2132      var bs = beanStore();
2133      var b = ThrownStore.create(bs).impl(parentContext() == null ? null : parentContext().getThrownStore());
2134      bs.createBeanFromMethod(ThrownStore.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, b).ifPresent(b::impl);
2135      return b.build();
2136   });
2137
2138   /**
2139    * The runtime {@link VarResolver} — wraps the bootstrap resolver and adds {@link Messages} and {@link Config} beans.
2140    *
2141    * <p>
2142    * The bootstrap {@link Config} is pulled from the {@code rawConfig} memoizer to avoid a circular
2143    * dependency: the runtime {@link Config} wraps the bootstrap config in a session backed by this resolver.
2144    * A bean-store override or {@code @Bean} factory method REPLACES the result.
2145    */
2146   private final Memoizer<VarResolver> varResolver = memoizer(() -> {
2147      var bs = beanStore();
2148      var b = getBootstrapVarResolver().copy()
2149         .bean(Messages.class, getMessages())
2150         .bean(Config.class, rawConfig.get());
2151      var override = bs.createBeanFromMethod(VarResolver.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, b).orElse(null);
2152      return nn(override) ? override : b.build();
2153   });
2154
2155   /**
2156    * Per-mixin {@link RestContext} sub-contexts, keyed by the mixin class.
2157    *
2158    * <p>
2159    * Built lazily (alongside {@link #restOperations}) and only on the host's {@link RestContext} &mdash; mixin
2160    * sub-contexts themselves never spawn further sub-contexts (the flat-inheritance rule: a mixin's
2161    * {@code @Rest(mixins=B)} discovers B as a mixin of the host, not as a nested mixin of A).
2162    *
2163    * <p>
2164    * Each sub-context is constructed with a {@link ContextKind.Mixin} {@link Args#kind() kind}, with its
2165    * {@code parentContext} pointing at this host context.  That parent-linkage drives the inheritance walk in
2166    * {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String)} for serializers, parsers, encoders, converters, response
2167    * processors, REST op args, guards, callLogger, debugEnablement, messages, varResolver tokens, etc.
2168    *
2169    * @since 10.0.0
2170    */
2171   private final Memoizer<Map<Class<?>,RestContext>> mixinContexts = memoizer(() -> {
2172      if (isMixinContextField())
2173         return Map.of();
2174      var out = new LinkedHashMap<Class<?>,RestContext>();
2175      for (var rm : getResolvedMixins()) {
2176         if (rm.type() == resourceClass())
2177            continue;
2178         RestContext mixinCtx;
2179         try {
2180            mixinCtx = buildMixinContext(rm);
2181         } catch (RuntimeException e) {
2182            throw e;
2183         } catch (Exception e) {
2184            throw rex(e, "Failed to construct mixin sub-context for {0}: {1}", rm.type().getName(), e.getMessage());
2185         }
2186         out.put(rm.type(), mixinCtx);
2187      }
2188      return u(out);
2189   });
2190
2191   /**
2192    * Builds a per-mixin sub-{@link RestContext} for the given mixin class.
2193    *
2194    * <p>
2195    * Instantiates the mixin resource via this host's bean store, constructs a new {@link RestContext} with
2196    * {@code parentContext=this} and a {@link ContextKind.Mixin} {@link Args#kind() kind}, and reflectively invokes
2197    * any {@code setContext(RestContext)} method on the mixin instance (mirroring the existing
2198    * {@code RestChildren.buildChildContext(...)} contract).  The host's {@link ServletConfig} is propagated
2199    * so the mixin sees the same servlet container settings.
2200    *
2201    * <p>
2202    * Mixin instance resolution: an importer-supplied {@code @Bean <MixinClass>} factory in the host's
2203    * bean store wins over reflective instantiation. When the host registers
2204    * {@code @Bean public MyMixin myMixin() { ... }} (or a Spring {@code @Bean} of the same type), that
2205    * pre-built instance is used as the mixin resource; otherwise the mixin is instantiated via
2206    * {@code beanStore.instantiate(mixinClass)} (no-arg / builder / injected-constructor path). This lets
2207    * convention-endpoint mixins (favicon, SEO, version, well-known) be configured by the importer using
2208    * their own builder before the mixin walk wires them in.
2209    */
2210   private RestContext buildMixinContext(ResolvedMixin rm) throws Exception {
2211      var mixinClass = rm.type();
2212      Object preBuilt = beanStore.getBean(mixinClass).orElse(null);
2213      final var mixinResource = preBuilt != null ? preBuilt : beanStore.instantiate(mixinClass);
2214      var args = new Args(mixinClass, this, builder.inner, () -> mixinResource, "", null, null, null, new ContextKind.Mixin(rm));
2215      var mixinCtx = new RestContext(args);
2216      var setCtx = ClassInfo.of(mixinResource).getMethod(x -> x.hasName("setContext") && x.hasParameterTypes(RestContext.class)).orElse(null);
2217      if (setCtx != null)
2218         setCtx.accessible().invoke(mixinResource, mixinCtx);
2219      return mixinCtx;
2220   }
2221
2222   /**
2223    * Returns the per-mixin {@link RestContext} sub-contexts indexed by mixin class.
2224    *
2225    * <p>
2226    * Returns an empty map when this context is itself a mixin sub-context, or when no mixins are declared.
2227    *
2228    * @return An unmodifiable map of mixin class to its sub-{@link RestContext}; never {@code null}.
2229    * @since 10.0.0
2230    */
2231   public Map<Class<?>,RestContext> getMixinContexts() { return mixinContexts.get(); }
2232
2233   /**
2234    * Returns the host-chosen mount prefix(es) declared on the {@code @Mixin} that produced this mixin
2235    * sub-context, for re-mounting the mixin's endpoints under host-chosen path(s).
2236    *
2237    * <p>
2238    * Resolved from {@link Mixin#path()} and {@link Mixin#paths()} (SVL-resolved, then normalized via the same
2239    * {@code pathToken} cleanup the bundled SVL-configurable mixins use — leading/trailing slashes and a trailing
2240    * {@code /*} wildcard stripped to a bare token).  {@link Mixin#path()} (single) and {@link Mixin#paths()}
2241    * (multi) are unioned; an empty result means "no host re-mount" (the mixin's own op paths apply unchanged).
2242    *
2243    * @return The normalized, de-duplicated mount-prefix tokens (no slashes), or an empty list when none; never {@code null}.
2244    * @since 10.0.0
2245    */
2246   public List<String> getMixinMountPrefixes() { return mixinMountPrefixes.get(); }
2247
2248   /**
2249    * Memoized normalized {@code @Mixin(path/paths)} mount prefixes (built once; zero per-request cost).
2250    */
2251   private final Memoizer<List<String>> mixinMountPrefixes = memoizer(() -> {
2252      var rm = resolvedMixinField();
2253      if (rm == null || rm.hasNoOverrides())
2254         return List.of();
2255      var m = rm.overrides();
2256      var out = new LinkedHashSet<String>();
2257      var vrs = getBootstrapVarResolver().createSession();
2258      if (StringUtils.isNotBlank(m.path()))
2259         addMountPrefix(out, vrs.resolve(m.path()));
2260      for (var p : m.paths())
2261         if (StringUtils.isNotBlank(p))
2262            addMountPrefix(out, vrs.resolve(p));
2263      return List.copyOf(out);
2264   });
2265
2266   /** Normalizes one path override to a bare token (strip leading/trailing slashes and a trailing {@code /*}). */
2267   private static void addMountPrefix(Set<String> out, String raw) {
2268      var s = trim(raw);
2269      if (s.endsWith("/*"))
2270         s = s.substring(0, s.length() - 2);
2271      // Strip leading and trailing slashes.
2272      while (s.startsWith("/"))
2273         s = s.substring(1);
2274      while (s.endsWith("/"))
2275         s = s.substring(0, s.length() - 1);
2276      if (! s.isEmpty())
2277         out.add(s);
2278   }
2279
2280   /**
2281    * The {@link RestOperations} for this resource — all {@link RestOpContext} instances built from
2282    * methods annotated with {@link RestOp @RestOp} (and related).
2283    *
2284    * <p>
2285    * Eagerly initialized in the constructor (via an explicit {@code .get()} call inside the try-catch block)
2286    * so that any {@link ServletException} surfaces at construction time rather than lazily on first request.
2287    * Subsequent calls return the cached instance.
2288    *
2289    * <p>
2290    * For mixin classes declared via {@link Rest#mixins() @Rest(mixins)}, each mixin's {@code @RestOp} methods
2291    * are bound to a per-mixin {@link RestContext} sub-context (see {@link #mixinContexts}) so that the
2292    * mixin's annotation chain inherits from the host's via {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String)}.
2293    * Mixin sub-contexts themselves never participate in mixin discovery &mdash; the {@code if (!isMixinContext)}
2294    * guard below short-circuits the flat-inheritance rule (a mixin's {@code @Rest(mixins=B)} is collected at
2295    * the host level, not nested under A).
2296    */
2297   @SuppressWarnings({
2298      "java:S3776", // High cognitive complexity is inherent in REST operation set initialization.
2299      "java:S1141"  // Nested try/catch blocks required for granular error reporting during initialization.
2300   })
2301   private final Memoizer<RestOperations> restOperations = memoizer(() -> safe(() -> {
2302      initializeFrameworkBeansForRestOps();
2303      var bs = beanStore();
2304      var b = RestOperations.create(bs);
2305      var ap = getMarshallingContext().getAnnotationProvider();
2306      var rci = ClassInfo.of(resource().get());
2307      if (!isMixinContextField()) {
2308         var contexts = mixinContexts.get();
2309         for (var e : contexts.entrySet()) {
2310            var mixinClass = e.getKey();
2311            var mixinCtx = e.getValue();
2312            addRestOperationsForClass(b, ap, ClassInfo.of(mixinClass), mixinCtx, mixinCtx::getResource);
2313         }
2314      }
2315      addRestOperationsForClass(b, ap, rci, this, this::getResource);
2316      var override = bs.createBeanFromMethod(RestOperations.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, b).orElse(null);
2317      return nn(override) ? override : b.build();
2318   }));
2319
2320   private void addRestOperationsForClass(RestOperations.Builder b, AnnotationProvider ap, ClassInfo classInfo, RestContext opContext, Supplier<Object> targetSupplier) throws ServletException {
2321      for (var mi : classInfo.getPublicMethods()) {
2322         var al = rstream(ap.find(mi)).filter(REST_OP_GROUP).collect(Collectors.toList());
2323         if (al.isEmpty()) {
2324            Predicate<MethodInfo> isRestAnnotatedInterface = x -> x.getDeclaringClass().isInterface()
2325               && nn(x.getDeclaringClass().getAnnotations(Rest.class).findFirst().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).orElse(null));
2326            mi.getMatchingMethods().stream().filter(isRestAnnotatedInterface).forEach(x -> al.add(AnnotationInfo.of(x, RestOpAnnotation.DEFAULT)));
2327         }
2328         if (!al.isEmpty()) {
2329            try {
2330               if (mi.isNotPublic())
2331                  throw servletException("@RestOp method {0}.{1} must be defined as public.", classInfo.inner().getName(), mi.getNameSimple());
2332               var roc = new RestOpContext(mi.inner(), opContext, targetSupplier);
2333               if ("RRPC".equals(roc.getHttpMethod())) {
2334                  RestOpContext roc2 = new RrpcRestOpContext(mi.inner(), opContext, targetSupplier);
2335                  b.add("GET", roc2).add("POST", roc2);
2336               } else {
2337                  b.add(roc);
2338               }
2339            } catch (Exception e) {
2340               throw servletException(e, "Problem occurred trying to initialize methods on class {0}", classInfo.inner().getName());
2341            }
2342         }
2343      }
2344   }
2345
2346   /**
2347    * Discovers all mixins declared on this resource's {@code @Rest} chain, normalized to {@link ResolvedMixin}.
2348    *
2349    * <p>
2350    * Reads both {@link Rest#mixins() bare-class} and {@link Rest#mixinDefs() rich &#64;Mixin} entries (bare first,
2351    * then {@code mixinDefs}), recursing into each mixin class's own {@code @Rest(mixins=)}/{@code @Rest(mixinDefs=)}
2352    * (the flat-inheritance rule still applies — nested mixins are collected at the host level).  First occurrence
2353    * of a class wins for ordering; a rich {@code @Mixin} <b>upgrades</b> an earlier bare entry for the same class
2354    * (a bare class is just {@code @Mixin(type=X)} with empty overrides, so upgrading only adds the host overrides).
2355    *
2356    * @return The ordered, de-duplicated normalized mixins, never {@code null}.
2357    */
2358   private Collection<ResolvedMixin> getResolvedMixins() {
2359      var out = new LinkedHashMap<Class<?>,ResolvedMixin>();
2360      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(PROPERTY_mixins).forEach(ai -> {
2361         for (var mixin : ai.inner().mixins())
2362            collectResolvedMixin(ResolvedMixin.ofBare(mixin), out);
2363         for (var def : ai.inner().mixinDefs())
2364            collectResolvedMixin(ResolvedMixin.ofDef(def), out);
2365      });
2366      return out.values();
2367   }
2368
2369   private void collectResolvedMixin(ResolvedMixin rm, LinkedHashMap<Class<?>,ResolvedMixin> out) {
2370      var type = rm.type();
2371      // type==null is defensive only — @Mixin.type() defaults to Object.class and a declarative @Mixin requires
2372      // a class literal, so it is never null in practice.  type==resourceClass() guards a host/mixin listing
2373      // itself (skip to avoid self-recursion).
2374      if (type == null || type == resourceClass())  // HTT: the null arm is not reachable via the annotation API.
2375         return;
2376      var existing = out.get(type);
2377      if (existing != null) {
2378         // Already discovered.  A rich @Mixin upgrades a bare entry (in place, preserving position); otherwise
2379         // the first occurrence wins and we do not recurse again.
2380         if (existing.hasNoOverrides() && ! rm.hasNoOverrides())
2381            out.put(type, rm);
2382         return;
2383      }
2384      out.put(type, rm);
2385      var r = type.getAnnotation(Rest.class);
2386      if (r != null) {
2387         for (var nested : r.mixins())
2388            collectResolvedMixin(ResolvedMixin.ofBare(nested), out);
2389         for (var nestedDef : r.mixinDefs())
2390            collectResolvedMixin(ResolvedMixin.ofDef(nestedDef), out);
2391      }
2392   }
2393
2394   /**
2395    * The {@link RestChildren} for this resource — child {@link RestContext} instances registered via
2396    * {@link Rest#children() @Rest(children)}.
2397    *
2398    * <p>
2399    * Reads child classes directly from the {@code @Rest(children)} annotation chain (parent-to-child),
2400    * deduplicates, instantiates each child via the bean store, and builds a {@link RestContext} for each.
2401    * Eagerly initialized in the constructor (via an explicit {@code .get()} call inside the try-catch
2402    * block) so that any construction failure surfaces at initialization time rather than lazily.
2403    */
2404   @SuppressWarnings({
2405      "java:S3776" // cognitive complexity acceptable for child-context construction
2406   })
2407   private final Memoizer<RestChildren> restChildren = memoizer(() -> safe(() -> {
2408      var bs = beanStore();
2409      var servletConfig = bs.getBean(ServletConfig.class).orElse(null);
2410      var b = RestChildren.create(this, bs, servletConfig);
2411
2412      // Collect child classes from @Rest(children) on the annotation chain (parent-to-child order).
2413      // Deduplicate so the same child class registered on both a parent and child annotation
2414      // doesn't create two contexts.
2415      var seen = new LinkedHashSet<Class<?>>();
2416      getRestAnnotations().forEach(ai -> seen.addAll(Arrays.asList(ai.inner().children())));
2417
2418      var lazy = isLazyChildren();
2419
2420      for (var rc2 : seen) {
2421         if (rc2 == resourceClass())
2422            continue;  // Guard against self-reference infinite loop.
2423         if (lazy) {
2424            // Lazy: register a routing stub now; defer full RestContext construction to first request.
2425            b.addLazy(rc2, "");
2426         } else {
2427            // Eager (default): build the full child RestContext immediately.
2428            b.add(RestChildren.buildChildContext(this, bs, servletConfig, rc2, null, ""));
2429         }
2430      }
2431
2432      // @Bean override — allows replacing the entire RestChildren instance.
2433      var override = bs.createBeanFromMethod(RestChildren.class, resource().get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod, b).orElse(null);
2434      return nn(override) ? override : b.build();
2435   }));
2436
2437   /**
2438    * Constructor.
2439    *
2440    * @param args The bootstrap arguments. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
2441    * @throws Exception If any initialization problems were encountered.
2442    * @since 10.0.0
2443    */
2444   public RestContext(Args args) throws Exception {
2445      this(new Builder(args));
2446   }
2447
2448   /**
2449    * Internal constructor.
2450    *
2451    * <p>
2452    * Privatized in the April 2026 refactor (2026-04-19). External callers must use
2453    * {@link #RestContext(Args)} instead.
2454    *
2455    * @param builder The builder containing the settings for this bean.
2456    * @throws Exception If any initialization problems were encountered.
2457    */
2458   @SuppressWarnings({
2459      "java:S3776", // High cognitive complexity is inherent in REST context constructor initialization.
2460      "java:S1141"  // Nested try/catch blocks required for granular error reporting during initialization.
2461   })
2462   private RestContext(Builder builder) throws Exception {
2463      super(builder);
2464
2465      startTime = Instant.now();
2466
2467      REGISTRY.put(builder.resourceClass, this);
2468
2469      BasicHttpException initExceptionTemp = null;
2470
2471      try {
2472         this.builder = builder;
2473
2474         parentContext = builder.parentContext;
2475         contextKind = builder.args.kind();
2476         isMixinContext = contextKind instanceof ContextKind.Mixin;
2477         resolvedMixin = (contextKind instanceof ContextKind.Mixin m) ? m.def() : null;
2478         resourceClass = builder.resourceClass;
2479         var rs = new ResourceSupplier(resourceClass, assertArgNotNull("resource", builder.args.resource()));
2480         resource = rs;
2481
2482         // Resolve the programmatic configuration builder.  Prefer the one carried on Args (set by
2483         // RestServlet.init()); otherwise read the builder stashed on the resource instance (non-reflective) so
2484         // programmatic construction via MockRestClient, child mounting, and mixin composition all honor it.
2485         var rb = builder.args.restBuilder();
2486         if (rb == null)
2487            rb = stashedRestBuilder(rs.get());
2488         restBuilder = rb;
2489         if (restBuilder instanceof AbstractRestBuilder<?,?> arb) {
2490            // Synthetic, highest-priority @Rest carrying the builder-set members; prepended (most-derived
2491            // child position) to the @Rest chain by the restAnnotations memoizer so builder values win.
2492            builderRestAnnotation = arb.toRestAnnotation();
2493            // Programmatic mdcAsyncPropagation knob (no @Rest member) wins over the env-driven default.
2494            if (arb.getMdcAsyncPropagation() != null && builder.mdcAsyncPropagation == null)
2495               builder.mdcAsyncPropagation = arb.getMdcAsyncPropagation();
2496         } else {
2497            builderRestAnnotation = null;
2498         }
2499
2500         // --- beanStore setup (May 2026 refactor; precedence-flipped 10.0) ---
2501
2502         // Determine the parent (bootstrap) store: inherited from parent resource if present.
2503         //
2504         // For mixin sub-contexts, the parent is the host's FULL beanStore (not just the bootstrap layer)
2505         // so that resource-class-level @Bean factory methods declared on the host class are visible to
2506         // the mixin's resolution chain.  Mixin endpoints resolved through the host's beanStore directly;
2507         // with per-mixin sub-contexts, the same effect is
2508         // achieved by parent-linking the mixin's beanStore to the host's full beanStore.
2509         //
2510         // For top-level resources and @Rest(children) sub-resources, the parent remains the bootstrap
2511         // layer (children are intentionally independent of the host's resource-class beans).
2512         WritableBeanStore parentBs;
2513         if (parentContext != null && isMixinContext)
2514            parentBs = parentContext.beanStore;
2515         else if (parentContext != null)
2516            parentBs = parentContext.bootstrapBeanStore;
2517         else
2518            parentBs = null;
2519
2520         // Build the initial beanStore; honor an optional @Bean WritableBeanStore override.
2521         // In the new 10.0 precedence model, the parent (Spring or parent-resource bootstrap) is
2522         // installed as the overriding parent so it wins over local entries.
2523         // Args.overridingParent threads into the per-resource (final beanStore) overridingParent
2524         // slot so test-time overlays (TestBeanStore) resolve at tier 1 of the chain ahead of
2525         // any @Bean factory result registered as a local entry below.
2526         // @formatter:off
2527         var argsOverlay = builder.args.overridingParent();
2528         WritableBeanStore bs = createBeanStore(parentBs, rs, parentBs == null ? null : argsOverlay)
2529            .addBean(ResourceSupplier.class, rs)
2530            .addBean(ServletConfig.class, nn(builder.inner) ? builder.inner : builder)
2531            .addBean(ServletContext.class, (nn(builder.inner) ? builder.inner : builder).getServletContext());
2532         // @formatter:on
2533
2534         // If no parent store, promote bs to bootstrap and layer a fresh per-resource v2 store on top.
2535         // The overlay belongs on the per-resource store so it wins over @Bean factories registered
2536         // as local entries below; the bootstrap deliberately does NOT get the overlay (it sits above
2537         // the per-resource entries via the parent chain, which is too low for an overlay).
2538         if (parentBs == null) {
2539            bootstrapBeanStore = bs;
2540            bs = new BasicBeanStore(bootstrapBeanStore, argsOverlay);
2541         } else {
2542            bootstrapBeanStore = parentBs;
2543         }
2544         beanStore = bs;
2545
2546         beanStore.addBean(WritableBeanStore.class, beanStore);
2547         // Register the bootstrap VarResolver as a NAMED entry only.  The unnamed VarResolver
2548         // slot is intentionally left to the registerFrameworkDefaults() default supplier (which
2549         // resolves to the full runtime VarResolver) so that internal accessors such as
2550         // getVarResolver() return the right thing when routed through beanStore.getBean(...).
2551         beanStore.add(VarResolver.class, getBootstrapVarResolver(), PROP_bootstrapVarResolver);
2552         // Force-build raw Config now (fail fast if @Rest(config) is misconfigured).  The unnamed
2553         // Config slot in the bean store is intentionally left to the default supplier (the full
2554         // runtime Config) — @RestInit hooks that take Config as a parameter will see the fully
2555         // resolved instance instead of the raw bootstrap Config (10.0 behavior change).
2556         rawConfig.get();
2557         // Per-RestContext @Value resolution bridge: register the @Rest(config=...) Configs as
2558         // PropertySource beans inside THIS resource's BeanStore (NOT on the process-wide
2559         // Settings.get() singleton — that path was a 6.7x perf regression because
2560         // MockRestClient's static RestContext cache leaks instances and Settings.get()'s source
2561         // list grew unbounded). Per-resource isolation comes for free from the BeanStore scope.
2562         registerRestConfigPropertySources();
2563
2564         // Register memoizer-backed defaults for every framework-managed type.  These sit at the
2565         // bottom of the precedence order and only fire when no @Bean method, no programmatic
2566         // add, and no Spring/overriding-parent bean has been registered for the type.  This is
2567         // what removes the need for the old DELAYED_INJECTION gate-keeping list — the @Bean
2568         // walk below can now invoke any framework type's factory and still resolve framework
2569         // dependencies through the bean store.
2570         registerFrameworkDefaults(beanStore);
2571
2572         // For mixin sub-contexts, the bean store is parent-linked to the host's full beanStore so that
2573         // host-declared @Bean factory results (e.g. @Bean(name="db") HealthIndicator dbIndicator()) are
2574         // visible through the mixin's lookup chain.  But the parent walk also picks up the host's
2575         // framework defaults (SerializerSet, ParserSet, CallLogger, ...) at the parent's tier-4 slot
2576         // before this store's tier-4 defaults can fire — which would shadow this mixin's per-context
2577         // framework objects.  Promoting our defaults into local entries (tier 2) makes them resolve
2578         // ahead of the parent walk while still letting an overriding parent (e.g. Spring) and explicit
2579         // local @Bean registrations win.  Top-level resources and @Rest(children=...) sub-resources keep
2580         // the original tier-4 default semantics so a parent-supplied framework bean (e.g. Spring's
2581         // SerializerSet) still wins as it did before.
2582         if (isMixinContext && beanStore instanceof BasicBeanStore mixinBs)
2583            mixinBs.promoteDefaultsToLocalSuppliers();
2584
2585         var rci2 = ClassInfo.of(resourceClass);
2586
2587         // Register @Bean fields that already have a value.
2588         // @formatter:off
2589         rci2.getAllFields().stream()
2590            .filter(x -> x.hasAnnotation(Bean.class))
2591            .forEach(x -> o(x.get(resource.get())).ifPresent(
2592               y -> beanStore.add(
2593                  x.getFieldType().inner(),
2594                  y,
2595                  BeanAnnotation.name(x.getAnnotations(Bean.class).findFirst().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).orElse(null))
2596               )
2597            ));
2598         // @formatter:on
2599
2600         // Run @Bean methods and register their results as LOCAL entries (level 2 of resolve()).
2601         //
2602         // For non-framework types: invoke the @Bean method directly via createBeanFromMethod
2603         // and store the result via addBean.
2604         //
2605         // For framework types (those with a default supplier registered above): the @Bean
2606         // scan already ran inside the corresponding memoizer body (see e.g. createCallLogger()),
2607         // so re-invoking createBeanFromMethod here would create a SECOND instance and produce
2608         // inconsistent state between the framework's memoizer-backed bean and the bean store's
2609         // local entry.  Instead, PROMOTE the existing default supplier (which is memoizer-backed
2610         // and resolves to the @Bean value when one was supplied) into a local-entry supplier.
2611         // Promoting at level 2 means @Bean results win over a parent (Spring) at level 3.
2612         //
2613         // Net effect: @Bean method results uniformly take precedence over Spring/parent
2614         // bindings for both framework and user-defined types.  This auto-derives the legacy
2615         // DELAYED_INJECTION list from the default-supplier registrations.
2616         rci2.getAllMethods().stream().filter(x -> x.hasAnnotation(Bean.class)).forEach(x -> {
2617            var rt = x.getReturnType().<Object>inner();
2618            var name = BeanAnnotation.name(x.getAnnotations(Bean.class).findFirst().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).orElse(null));
2619            // Skip the WritableBeanStore factory (already consumed by createBeanStore()).
2620            if (WritableBeanStore.class.equals(rt) || BeanStore.class.equals(rt))
2621               return;
2622            if (beanStore instanceof BasicBeanStore bbs2 && bbs2.hasDefaultSupplier(rt, name)) {
2623               bbs2.getDefaultSupplier(rt, name).ifPresent(sup -> beanStore.addSupplier(rt, sup, name));
2624               return;
2625            }
2626            beanStore.createBeanFromMethod(rt, resource.get(), RestContext::isBeanMethod)
2627               .ifPresent(y -> beanStore.addBean(rt, y, name));
2628         });
2629
2630         // Run @RestInit-annotated methods on the resource object (deduplicated by signature, top-down order).
2631         // Note: pre-10.0 this filter also excluded `y.hasParameter(RestOpContext.Builder.class)` because the
2632         // per-op `@RestInit(RestOpContext.Builder)` flow handled those separately. That flow was deleted in
2633         // April 2026 refactor Route B (zero real-world callers), so the exclusion is gone too. Likewise, the
2634         // class-level `@RestInit(RestContext.Builder)` injection protocol was deleted in the same refactor
2635         // (zero non-test callers anywhere) — `RestContext.Builder` is no longer added to the bean store, so
2636         // any straggling `@RestInit` method that still declares either Builder type as a parameter will now
2637         // surface a "missing prerequisites" error here, which is the desired loud-failure signal.
2638         var r = resource.get();
2639         var initMap = CollectionUtils.<String,MethodInfo>map();
2640         ClassInfo.ofProxy(r).getAllMethodsTopDown().stream()
2641            .filter(y -> y.hasAnnotation(RestInit.class))
2642            .forEach(y -> { var sig = y.getSignature(); if (!initMap.containsKey(sig)) initMap.put(sig, y.accessible()); });
2643         for (var m : initMap.values()) {
2644            if (!m.canResolveAllParameters(beanStore, r))
2645               throw servletException("Could not call @RestInit method {0}.{1}.  Could not find prerequisites: {2}.", cns(m.getDeclaringClass()), m.getSignature(), m.getMissingParameterTypes(beanStore, r));
2646            try {
2647               m.inject(beanStore, r);
2648            } catch (Exception e) {
2649               throw servletException(e, "Exception thrown from @RestInit method {0}.{1}.", cns(m.getDeclaringClass()), m.getSignature());
2650            }
2651         }
2652
2653         // Back-fill @Bean fields that were null before init hooks ran.
2654         // @formatter:off
2655         rci2.getAllFields().stream()
2656            .filter(x -> x.hasAnnotation(Bean.class))
2657            .forEach(x -> x.setIfNull(
2658               resource.get(),
2659               beanStore.getBean(
2660                  x.getFieldType().inner(),
2661                  BeanAnnotation.name(x.getAnnotations(Bean.class).findFirst().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).orElse(null))
2662               ).orElse(null)
2663            ));
2664         // @formatter:on
2665
2666         // Populate @Inject-annotated fields and methods on the resource, then fire @PostConstruct
2667         // callbacks.  This covers the JSR-330 / Spring @Autowired / jakarta.inject.Inject FQNs as
2668         // well as the Juneau-owned @Inject in commons.inject — recognition is FQN-based, see JsrSupport.
2669         rci2.inject(resource.get(), beanStore);
2670
2671         builder.args.beanStoreConfigurer().accept(beanStore);
2672
2673         // --- end beanStore setup ---
2674
2675         // Path resolution: explicit Args.path (non-empty) wins so callers like
2676         // RestChildren.addChild(String,Object) can mount the same class at a custom path; otherwise read
2677         // from the @Rest(path) annotation chain (most-derived class wins). This restores override behavior
2678         // removed in the May 2026 Builder.path elimination, without resurrecting the staging field.
2679         var argsPath = builder.args.path();
2680         if (argsPath != null && ! argsPath.isEmpty()) {
2681            path = trimLeadingSlashes(argsPath);
2682         } else {
2683            path = getRestAnnotations().stream()
2684               .map(ai -> ai.inner().path())
2685               .filter(StringUtils::isNotEmpty)
2686               .findFirst()
2687               .map(s -> trimLeadingSlashes(s))
2688               .orElse("");
2689         }
2690         fullPath = (parentContext == null ? "" : (parentContext.fullPath + '/')) + path;
2691         var p = path;
2692         if (! p.endsWith("/*"))
2693            p += "/*";
2694         pathMatcher = UrlPathMatcher.of(p);
2695
2696         // Top-level mount-paths resolution chain (programmatic > getter > annotation default).
2697         // See javadoc on getPaths() for the full precedence order. Resolution happens once at construction;
2698         // the resolved array is what hosting runtimes (Jetty auto-discovery, Spring Boot ServletRegistrationBean)
2699         // should consume rather than reading the raw @Rest(paths) annotation directly.  Each @Rest(paths)
2700         // element is SVL-resolved through a VarResolver session backed by the live bean store — so
2701         // $C{key} consults whatever Config is currently registered (test overlay, @Bean factory, or the
2702         // framework's runtime Config) and $E{NAME,default} / $S{prop,default} use the bootstrap variable
2703         // catalog without needing any beans.  The session-with-beanstore form is what makes test-time
2704         // Config injection visible to SVL without firing the full runtime VarResolver memoizer.
2705         paths = resolveMountPaths(builder, resource.get(), getBootstrapVarResolver(), beanStore, getRestAnnotations());
2706
2707         // Build annotation work list, then trigger beanContextBuilder (which applies it).
2708         var vrs = getBootstrapVarResolver().createSession();
2709         annotationWork = AnnotationWorkList.of(vrs, rstream(AnnotationProvider.INSTANCE.find(rci2)).filter(CONTEXT_APPLY_FILTER));
2710         beanContextBuilder.get(); // force init with annotationWork now set
2711
2712         // @formatter:off
2713         beanStore
2714            .addBean(RestContext.class, this)
2715            .addBean(Object.class, resource.get())
2716            .addBean(Builder.class, builder)
2717            .addBean(AnnotationWorkList.class, annotationWork);
2718         // @formatter:on
2719
2720      // Inject @Value-annotated env-driven defaults onto this RestContext instance so that the
2721      // memoizer lambdas below (which run lazily on first .get() call) read fully resolved values.
2722      // Must run BEFORE isEagerInit() since the eagerInit memoizer itself reads defaultEagerInit.
2723      ClassInfo.of(this).inject(this, beanStore);
2724
2725      // Startup-fail if @Rest(observability="true") is set but neither MetricsRecorder nor TracerHook is
2726      // supplied. The check runs after all @Bean method injection so that consumer-provided beans are visible.
2727      checkObservabilityBackendPresent();
2728
2729      // Startup-fail: if asyncCompletionExecutor is configured (either by annotation or
2730      // programmatically), force-evaluate the memoizer now so "bean not found" surfaces at context
2731      // init time rather than lazily on the first request.
2732      checkAsyncCompletionExecutorPresent();
2733
2734      if (isEagerInit()) {
2735            // Force-fire the framework-bean memoizers in dependency-friendly order so their @Rest()
2736            // annotation walks (e.g. `@Rest(partParser=…)`, `@Rest(partSerializer=…)`, `@Rest(encoders=…)`,
2737            // etc.) execute eagerly inside the try-catch.  These walks MUTATE the cached creators that
2738            // downstream RestOpContext memoizers later copy from, so they must run before
2739            // getRestOperations() builds the per-op contexts.  We do NOT re-add results to the bean
2740            // store — the default suppliers registered earlier already cover lookups.
2741            initializeFrameworkBeansForRestOps();
2742
2743            // Force-initialize restOperations and restChildren now so that any construction failures
2744            // (e.g. bad @RestOp method or invalid child class) surface here inside the try-catch.
2745            getRestOperations();
2746            getRestChildren();
2747         }
2748
2749         // produces/consumes are resolved lazily via the produces/consumes memoizers below
2750         // (April 2026 refactor, 2026-04-19) — they walk the @Rest(produces=...) / @Rest(consumes=...)
2751         // chain first, then fall back to deriving the intersection of opContexts' supported media types.
2752
2753      } catch (BasicHttpException e) {
2754         initExceptionTemp = e;
2755         throw e;
2756      } catch (Exception e) {
2757         initExceptionTemp = new InternalServerError(e);
2758         throw e;
2759      } finally {
2760         initException = initExceptionTemp;
2761      }
2762   }
2763
2764   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
2765   public RestSession.Builder createSession() {
2766      return RestSession.create(this);
2767   }
2768
2769   //---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2770   // Memoized allowlist fields
2771   //---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2772
2773   /**
2774    * Memoized parser session-option keys from {@code @Rest(allowedParserOptions)}, after SVL resolution and comma expansion.
2775    *
2776    * <p>
2777    * When inheritance is not blocked, keys from {@link #parentContext} are included first, then this resource's own tokens.
2778    * Leading-hyphen tokens (e.g. {@code -foo}) remove earlier positive tokens.
2779    */
2780   private final Memoizer<SortedSet<String>> allowedParserOptions = memoizer(() -> {
2781      var l = new ArrayList<String>();
2782      var p = PROPERTY_allowedParserOptions;
2783      var pc = parentContext();
2784      if (isInherited(p) && pc != null)
2785         l.addAll(pc.getAllowedParserOptions());
2786      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(p).forEach(x -> resolveCdl(x.getStringArray(p)).forEach(l::add));
2787      return u(treeSetCi(removeNegations(l)));
2788   });
2789
2790   /**
2791    * Memoized serializer session-option keys from {@code @Rest(allowedSerializerOptions)}, after SVL resolution and comma expansion.
2792    *
2793    * <p>
2794    * When inheritance is not blocked, keys from {@link #parentContext} are included first, then this resource's own tokens.
2795    * Leading-hyphen tokens remove earlier positive tokens.
2796    */
2797   private final Memoizer<SortedSet<String>> allowedSerializerOptions = memoizer(() -> {
2798      var l = new ArrayList<String>();
2799      var p = PROPERTY_allowedSerializerOptions;
2800      var pc = parentContext();
2801      if (isInherited(p) && pc != null)
2802         l.addAll(pc.getAllowedSerializerOptions());
2803      getRestAnnotationsForProperty(p).forEach(x -> resolveCdl(x.getStringArray(p)).forEach(l::add));
2804      return u(treeSetCi(removeNegations(l)));
2805   });
2806
2807   /**
2808    * Memoized value of the {@code noInherit} annotation attribute from the nearest {@code @Rest} annotation.
2809    *
2810    * <p>
2811    * {@code noInherit} itself is never inherited; it only applies to the {@code @Rest} that declares it.
2812    */
2813   private final Memoizer<SortedSet<String>> noInherit = memoizer(() -> {
2814      var out = treeSet(String.CASE_INSENSITIVE_ORDER,
2815         getRestAnnotation()
2816            .map(x -> x.getStringArray("noInherit").orElse(StringUtils.EMPTY_STRING_ARRAY))
2817            .map(x -> resolveCdl(x).toList())
2818            .orElseGet(Collections::emptyList));
2819      // Dual noInherit source: when this is a mixin sub-context built from a host @Mixin(noInherit=...),
2820      // the host-declared cutoff tokens union with the mixin class's own @Rest(noInherit=...).  A token from
2821      // EITHER source cuts the host→mixin inheritance walk for that property (OQ2/OQ3).
2822      var rmNoInherit = resolvedMixinField();
2823      if (rmNoInherit != null)
2824         resolveCdl(rmNoInherit.overrides().noInherit()).forEach(out::add);
2825      return out;
2826   });
2827
2828   /**
2829    * The host-declared {@code @Mixin} override values translated into a synthetic {@link Rest} annotation, for a
2830    * mixin sub-context that carries overrides.  {@code null} for non-mixin contexts and for mixins with no
2831    * overrides (bare classes).
2832    *
2833    * <p>
2834    * Built once at memoizer-init time (zero per-request cost).  The synthetic annotation is injected at the
2835    * most-derived position of {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String)} so the host override resolves as if
2836    * declared on the mixin class itself — list-shaped props append after the inherited host chain, replace-shaped
2837    * props win — but ahead of the mixin class's own {@code @Rest}, so the host override is authoritative (OQ3).
2838    */
2839   private final Memoizer<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> mixinOverrideAnnotation = memoizer(() -> {
2840      var rm = resolvedMixinField();
2841      if (rm == null || rm.hasNoOverrides())
2842         return null;
2843      var rest = buildMixinOverrideRest(rm.overrides());
2844      return AnnotationInfo.of(ClassInfo.of(getResourceClass()), rest);
2845   });
2846
2847   /**
2848    * Translates a host-declared {@link Mixin @Mixin} into a synthetic {@link Rest @Rest} carrying only its
2849    * override slots, so the standard {@code @Rest} resolution chain applies the overrides uniformly.
2850    *
2851    * @param m The {@code @Mixin} override declaration.
2852    * @return A synthetic {@code @Rest} populated from {@code m}'s override slots.
2853    */
2854   private static Rest buildMixinOverrideRest(Mixin m) {
2855      // @formatter:off
2856      var b = RestAnnotation.create()
2857         .guards(m.guards())
2858         .roleGuard(m.roleGuard())
2859         .rolesDeclared(m.rolesDeclared())
2860         .converters(m.converters())
2861         .encoders(m.encoders())
2862         .serializers(m.serializers())
2863         .parsers(m.parsers())
2864         .responseProcessors(m.responseProcessors())
2865         .restOpArgs(m.restOpArgs())
2866         .callLogger(m.callLogger())
2867         .partSerializer(m.partSerializer())
2868         .partParser(m.partParser())
2869         .debug(m.debug())
2870         .messages(m.messages())
2871         .defaultRequestHeaders(m.defaultRequestHeaders())
2872         .defaultResponseHeaders(m.defaultResponseHeaders())
2873         .defaultRequestAttributes(m.defaultRequestAttributes())
2874         .produces(m.produces())
2875         .consumes(m.consumes())
2876         .defaultAccept(m.defaultAccept())
2877         .defaultContentType(m.defaultContentType())
2878         .defaultCharset(m.defaultCharset())
2879         .maxInput(m.maxInput());
2880      // @formatter:on
2881      return b.build();
2882   }
2883
2884   /**
2885    * Memoized list of every {@link Rest} annotation on the resource class and its supertypes, in child-to-parent order.
2886    *
2887    * <p>
2888    * For <b>host</b> contexts (top-level resources), {@link DefaultConfig} is always synthesized as the top-most
2889    * (parent-most) entry when not already present in the class hierarchy. This makes {@code DefaultConfig.@Rest(...)}
2890    * the framework's single source of truth for default lists (response processors, REST op args, encoders, parsers,
2891    * serializers, ...), while still letting bare {@code @Rest}-annotated resources inherit the framework defaults
2892    * without explicitly implementing {@link BasicUniversalConfig} or any other {@code DefaultConfig} descendant.
2893    * Resources that <i>do</i> implement {@code DefaultConfig} (transitively or directly) won't get a duplicate
2894    * entry — the list is dedup'd by the {@link Class} the annotation was declared on.
2895    *
2896    * <p>
2897    * For <b>mixin</b> sub-contexts the synthesized {@code DefaultConfig} fallback is omitted: the mixin's annotation
2898    * chain is composed with the host's chain by
2899    * {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String) getRestAnnotationsForProperty(...)}, and the host's chain already
2900    * supplies the {@code DefaultConfig} fallback (synthesized or transitive). Including it on the mixin too would
2901    * place a second {@code DefaultConfig} entry between the host's most-derived {@code @Rest} and the mixin's own
