2902    * annotations in the combined parent-to-child walk, which would let the framework defaults silently override
2903    * a host's explicit per-property values for any property the mixin doesn't itself declare (e.g.
2904    * {@code partSerializer}, {@code partParser}).
2905    */
2906   private final Memoizer<List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>> restAnnotations = memoizer(() -> prependBuilderRestAnnotation(computeRawRestAnnotations()));
2907
2908   /**
2909    * Computes the raw {@code @Rest} annotation chain (most-derived first), folding in the framework
2910    * {@code DefaultConfig} annotations for non-mixin contexts.  Does not include the synthetic builder annotation.
2911    *
2912    * @return The raw annotation chain.
2913    */
2914   private List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> computeRawRestAnnotations() {
2915      var raw = getAnnotationProvider().find(Rest.class, ClassInfo.of(getResourceClass()));
2916      if (isMixinContextField())
2917         return raw;
2918      var hasDefaultConfig = raw.stream().anyMatch(ai -> ai.getAnnotatable() instanceof ClassInfo ci && DefaultConfig.class.equals(ci.inner()));
2919      if (hasDefaultConfig)
2920         return raw;
2921      var defaultConfigAnnotations = getAnnotationProvider().find(Rest.class, ClassInfo.of(DefaultConfig.class));
2922      if (defaultConfigAnnotations.isEmpty())
2923         return raw;
2924      var combined = new ArrayList<>(raw);
2925      combined.addAll(defaultConfigAnnotations);
2926      return Collections.unmodifiableList(combined);
2927   }
2928
2929   /**
2930    * Prepends the synthetic builder-supplied {@code @Rest} annotation at the most-derived (child)
2931    * position so its set members win in both child-first ({@code findFirst}) and parent-to-child
2932    * ({@code reduce-last}) resolution walks.  Returns {@code base} unchanged when there is no programmatic builder.
2933    *
2934    * @param base The raw annotation chain.
2935    * @return The chain with the synthetic annotation prepended (when present).
2936    */
2937   private List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> prependBuilderRestAnnotation(List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> base) {
2938      if (builderRestAnnotation == null)
2939         return base;
2940      var combined = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>(base.size() + 1);
2941      combined.add(AnnotationInfo.of(ClassInfo.of(getResourceClass()), builderRestAnnotation));
2942      combined.addAll(base);
2943      return Collections.unmodifiableList(combined);
2944   }
2945
2946   /**
2947    * Non-reflectively reads the programmatic configuration builder stashed on a resource/mixin instance,
2948    * or returns <jk>null</jk> when the instance carries none or is not a builder-aware base type.
2949    *
2950    * @param r The resource instance.
2951    * @return The stashed builder, or <jk>null</jk>.
2952    */
2953   private static RestBuilder<?> stashedRestBuilder(Object r) {
2954      if (r instanceof RestServlet x)
2955         return x.getRestBuilder();
2956      if (r instanceof RestResource x)
2957         return x.getRestBuilder();
2958      if (r instanceof RestMixin x)
2959         return x.getRestBuilder();
2960      return null;
2961   }
2962
2963   /**
2964    * The {@code @Rest} annotation list for this resource in parent-to-child (top-down) order.
2965    *
2966    * <p>
2967    * This is the reverse of {@link #restAnnotations}: most-ancestor annotation first, most-derived last.
2968    * Use this when walking the annotation chain in inheritance order so that child values append after or
2969    * override parent values.
2970    */
2971   private final Memoizer<List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>> restAnnotationsTopDown = memoizer(() -> {
2972      var list = new ArrayList<>(getRestAnnotations());
2973      Collections.reverse(list);
2974      return Collections.unmodifiableList(list);
2975   });
2976
2977   private Stream<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(String propertyName) {
2978      return getRestAnnotationsForProperty(propertyName).sorted(Comparator.comparingInt(AnnotationInfo::getRank));
2979   }
2980
2981   private static String leadingEnumToken(String s) {
2982      s = trim(s);
2983      if (isEmpty(s))
2984         return s;
2985      int i = 0;
2986      while (i < s.length() && (Character.isLetter(s.charAt(i)) || s.charAt(i) == '_'))
2987         i++;
2988      return s.substring(0, i);
2989   }
2990
2991   private <E extends Enum<E>> Optional<E> parseEnumConstant(Class<E> enumClass, String resolved) {
2992      var t = leadingEnumToken(trim(emptyIfNull(resolved)));
2993      if (isEmpty(t))
2994         return oe();
2995      try {
2996         return o(Enum.valueOf(enumClass, t));
2997      } catch (IllegalArgumentException ignored) {
2998         return oe();
2999      }
3000   }
3001
3002   String mergeReplacedStringAttribute(String propertyName, String initialFromEnvOrNull) {
3003      var v = new AtomicReference<>(initialFromEnvOrNull == null ? null : resolve(initialFromEnvOrNull));
3004      restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(propertyName).forEach(ai ->
3005         ai.getString(propertyName)
3006            .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3007            .map(this::resolve)
3008            .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3009            .ifPresent(v::set));
3010      return v.get();
3011   }
3012
3013   private boolean mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(String propertyName, boolean envDefault) {
3014      var v = new AtomicReference<>(envDefault);
3015      restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(propertyName).forEach(ai ->
3016         ai.getString(propertyName)
3017            .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3018            .map(this::resolve)
3019            .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3020            .ifPresent(s -> v.set(Boolean.parseBoolean(s))));
3021      return v.get();
3022   }
3023
3024   /**
3025    * Header names that may be passed via URL query parameter; resolved from {@code @Rest(allowedHeaderParams)},
3026    * default {@code "Accept,Content-Type"}.
3027    */
3028   private final Memoizer<Set<String>> allowedHeaderParams = memoizer(() ->
3029      Collections.unmodifiableSet(newCaseInsensitiveSet(mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_allowedHeaderParams, defaultAllowedHeaderParams))));
3030
3031   /**
3032    * HTTP method names that may be specified via a request header; resolved from {@code @Rest(allowedMethodHeaders)},
3033    * default empty.
3034    */
3035   private final Memoizer<Set<String>> allowedMethodHeaders = memoizer(() ->
3036      Collections.unmodifiableSet(newCaseInsensitiveSet(mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_allowedMethodHeaders, defaultAllowedMethodHeaders))));
3037
3038   /**
3039    * HTTP method names that may be specified via URL query parameter; resolved from {@code @Rest(allowedMethodParams)},
3040    * default {@code "HEAD,OPTIONS"}.
3041    */
3042   private final Memoizer<Set<String>> allowedMethodParams = memoizer(() ->
3043      Collections.unmodifiableSet(newCaseInsensitiveSet(mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_allowedMethodParams, defaultAllowedMethodParams))));
3044
3045   /**
3046    * Whether a {@code &content=} URL parameter may override the request body; inverse of
3047    * {@code @Rest(disableContentParam)}.
3048    */
3049   private final Memoizer<Boolean> allowContentParam = memoizer(() ->
3050      !mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_disableContentParam, defaultDisableContentParam));
3051
3052   /**
3053    * Whether exception stack traces are rendered in error responses; resolved from
3054    * {@code @Rest(renderResponseStackTraces)}.
3055    */
3056   private final Memoizer<Boolean> renderResponseStackTraces = memoizer(() ->
3057      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_renderResponseStackTraces, defaultRenderResponseStackTraces));
3058
3059   /**
3060    * Whether the resource emits RFC 7807 {@code application/problem+json} responses; resolved from
3061    * {@code @Rest(problemDetails)}.
3062    */
3063   private final Memoizer<Boolean> problemDetails = memoizer(() ->
3064      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_problemDetails, defaultProblemDetails));
3065
3066   /**
3067    * Whether the resource opts into per-request virtual-thread dispatch on Java 21+; resolved from
3068    * {@code @Rest(virtualThreads)}.
3069    *
3070    * <p>
3071    * Detection happens at context-init via {@link #mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(String, boolean)}; on JVMs older
3072    * than Java 21 the flag is logged once and ignored — see {@link #virtualThreadExecutor}.
3073    */
3074   private final Memoizer<Boolean> virtualThreadsEnabled = memoizer(() ->
3075      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_virtualThreads, defaultVirtualThreads));
3076
3077   /**
3078    * Resource-level observability tri-state from {@code @Rest(observability)}: {@code "true"} (strict opt-in),
3079    * {@code "false"} (explicit opt-out), or {@code null} / empty (default inherit behavior).
3080    *
3081    * <p>
3082    * Per-operation overrides (from verb / {@code @RestOp} annotations) are resolved independently in
3083    * {@link RestOpContext}.
3084    */
3085   private final Memoizer<String> observabilityAttribute = memoizer(() ->
3086      mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_observability, null));
3087
3088   /**
3089    * Configurable async-response timeout (milliseconds) applied by {@code AsyncResponseProcessor} to
3090    * {@link CompletableFuture}-returning handlers; resolved from {@code @Rest(asyncTimeoutMillis)}.
3091    *
3092    * <p>
3093    * {@code 0} disables the timeout. The default 30-second fallback is applied by {@code AsyncResponseProcessor}
3094    * itself when neither the resource nor the operation declares a value.
3095    */
3096   private final Memoizer<Long> asyncTimeoutMillis = memoizer(() -> {
3097      var s = mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_asyncTimeoutMillis, null);
3098      if (isEmpty(s))
3099         return -1L;
3100      try {
3101         return Long.parseLong(s.trim());
3102      } catch (NumberFormatException nfe) {
3103         ASYNC_LOG.log(Level.WARNING, () -> "Invalid @Rest(asyncTimeoutMillis) value '" + s + "' — falling back to default.");
3104         return -1L;
3105      }
3106   });
3107
3108   /**
3109    * The async-completion executor for this resource, resolved by name from the bean store; {@code null} when
3110    * {@code @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)} is blank (the common case).
3111    *
3112    * <p>
3113    * The memoizer throws {@link IllegalStateException} at first access if the bean name was supplied but no
3114    * matching {@link Executor} bean is found — this surfaces as a startup failure, not a silent no-op.
3115    */
3116   private final Memoizer<Executor> asyncCompletionExecutor = memoizer(() -> {
3117      // Programmatic override takes priority over the annotation chain.
3118      var name = resolveAsyncCompletionExecutorName();
3119      if (isBlank(name))
3120         return null;
3121      var resolved = beanStore().getBean(Executor.class, name);
3122      // Try ExecutorService as well (a common supertype users supply that is-a Executor).
3123      if (resolved.isEmpty())
3124         resolved = beanStore().getBean(ExecutorService.class, name).map(e -> (Executor) e);
3125      if (resolved.isEmpty())
3126         throw new IllegalStateException(
3127            "@Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor) on " + getResourceClass().getName()
3128               + " references bean name '" + name + "' but no Executor (or ExecutorService) bean"
3129               + " with that name is registered in the resource's bean store.");
3130      return resolved.get();
3131   });
3132
3133   // Extracted to a method so the compiler does not flag a blank-final-field access inside a field-initializer lambda.
3134   private String resolveAsyncCompletionExecutorName() {
3135      return builder.asyncCompletionExecutorName != null
3136         ? builder.asyncCompletionExecutorName
3137         : mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_asyncCompletionExecutor, null);
3138   }
3139
3140   /**
3141    * Lazily-instantiated virtual-thread executor used by {@link RestOpInvoker} when
3142    * {@code @Rest(virtualThreads=true)} is set on this resource and the runtime is Java 21+.
3143    *
3144    * <p>
3145    * On Java 17/18/19/20 the supplier returns {@code null} and emits a one-shot {@code WARNING} log so the
3146    * resource degrades gracefully to caller-thread dispatch.
3147    */
3148   private final Memoizer<Executor> virtualThreadExecutor = memoizer(() -> {
3149      // NOTE: Intentionally not gated on resource-level {@code virtualThreadsEnabled.get()} — per-op
3150      // {@code @RestOp(virtualThreads="true")} can opt in even when the enclosing {@code @Rest} doesn't.
3151      // Callers ({@link RestOpInvoker#invokeOp}) only reach this method when the effective op-level flag is true,
3152      // so the warning emitted on Java &lt;21 is appropriate at first call regardless of where the flag is set.
3153      if (Runtime.version().feature() < 21) {
3154         ASYNC_LOG.log(Level.WARNING, () -> "virtualThreads=true configured on " + getResourceClass().getName()
3155            + " but runtime is Java " + Runtime.version().feature() + " — virtual-thread dispatch requires Java 21+. "
3156            + "Falling back to caller-thread dispatch.");
3157         return null;
3158      }
3159      try {
3160         var m = Executors.class.getMethod("newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor");
3161         return (Executor) m.invoke(null);
3162      } catch (ReflectiveOperationException e) {
3163         ASYNC_LOG.log(Level.WARNING, e, () -> "Reflective creation of virtual-thread executor failed on "
3164            + getResourceClass().getName() + " — falling back to caller-thread dispatch.");
3165         return null;
3166      }
3167   });
3168
3169   /** Logger for async / virtual-thread setup events. Used at memoizer init before {@link #getLogger()} may be wired up. */
3170   private static final Logger ASYNC_LOG = Logger.getLogger(RestContext.class.getName() + ".async");
3171
3172   /**
3173    * Whether framework memoizers and operation/child contexts should be force-initialized during constructor execution;
3174    * resolved from {@code @Rest(eagerInit)}.
3175    */
3176   private final Memoizer<Boolean> eagerInit = memoizer(() ->
3177      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_eagerInit, defaultEagerInit));
3178
3179   /**
3180    * Annotation + env-driven component of the lazy-children flag.
3181    *
3182    * <p>
3183    * Reads the {@link Rest#lazyChildren() @Rest(lazyChildren)} annotation chain and falls back to the
3184    * {@code RestContext.lazyChildren} env-driven default.  The programmatic
3185    * {@link Builder#lazyChildInit(boolean)} knob is applied in {@link #isLazyChildren()} instead of here
3186    * because blank-final-field rules prevent the memoizer lambda from safely capturing {@link #builder}.
3187    */
3188   private final Memoizer<Boolean> lazyChildrenAnnotation = memoizer(() ->
3189      mergeReplacedBooleanAttribute(PROPERTY_lazyChildren, defaultLazyChildren));
3190
3191   /**
3192    * The request header used for client-version matching; resolved from {@code @Rest(clientVersionHeader)},
3193    * default {@code "Client-Version"}.
3194    */
3195   private final Memoizer<String> clientVersionHeader = memoizer(() ->
3196      mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_clientVersionHeader, defaultClientVersionHeader));
3197
3198   /**
3199    * The {@link UriRelativity} strategy for URI resolution in this resource.
3200    *
3201    * <p>
3202    * Resolved from {@code @Rest(uriRelativity)} annotations (most-derived non-blank wins), with env-var
3203    * fallback. Defaults to {@link UriRelativity#RESOURCE}.
3204    */
3205   private final Memoizer<UriRelativity> uriRelativity = memoizer(() -> {
3206      var v = new AtomicReference<>(
3207         parseEnumConstant(UriRelativity.class, resolve(emptyIfNull(defaultUriRelativity)))
3208            .orElse(UriRelativity.RESOURCE)
3209      );
3210      restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(PROPERTY_uriRelativity).forEach(ai -> ai.getString(PROPERTY_uriRelativity).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).ifPresent(s ->
3211         parseEnumConstant(UriRelativity.class, resolve(s)).ifPresent(v::set)
3212      ));
3213      return v.get();
3214   });
3215
3216   /**
3217    * The URI authority (scheme + host + port) override for this resource.
3218    *
3219    * <p>
3220    * Resolved from {@code @Rest(uriAuthority)} annotations and env-var; inherited from parent when not
3221    * blocked by {@code noInherit}. {@code null} means no override.
3222    */
3223   private final Memoizer<String> uriAuthority = memoizer(() -> {
3224      String local = mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_uriAuthority, defaultUriAuthority.orElse(null));
3225      if (nn(local))
3226         return local;
3227      var pc = parentContext();
3228      return isInherited(PROPERTY_uriAuthority) && pc != null ? pc.getUriAuthority() : null;
3229   });
3230
3231   /**
3232    * The URI context path override for this resource.
3233    *
3234    * <p>
3235    * Resolved from {@code @Rest(uriContext)} annotations and env-var; inherited from parent when not
3236    * blocked by {@code noInherit}. {@code null} means no override.
3237    */
3238   private final Memoizer<String> uriContext = memoizer(() -> {
3239      String local = mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_uriContext, defaultUriContext.orElse(null));
3240      if (nn(local))
3241         return local;
3242      var pc = parentContext();
3243      return isInherited(PROPERTY_uriContext) && pc != null ? pc.getUriContext() : null;
3244   });
3245
3246   /**
3247    * The {@link UriResolution} strategy for URI resolution in this resource.
3248    *
3249    * <p>
3250    * Resolved from {@code @Rest(uriResolution)} annotations (most-derived non-blank wins), with env-var
3251    * fallback. Defaults to {@link UriResolution#ROOT_RELATIVE}.
3252    */
3253   private final Memoizer<UriResolution> uriResolution = memoizer(() -> {
3254      var v = new AtomicReference<>(
3255         parseEnumConstant(UriResolution.class, resolve(emptyIfNull(defaultUriResolution)))
3256            .orElse(UriResolution.ROOT_RELATIVE)
3257      );
3258      restAnnotationsForPropertySortedByRank(PROPERTY_uriResolution).forEach(ai -> ai.getString(PROPERTY_uriResolution).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).ifPresent(s ->
3259         parseEnumConstant(UriResolution.class, resolve(s)).ifPresent(v::set)
3260      ));
3261      return v.get();
3262   });
3263
3264   /**
3265    * Property names that describe how a class is mounted on the URL tree, and therefore must NOT inherit from a
3266    * parent context when walking annotations on a {@linkplain #isMixinContext() mixin sub-context}.
3267    *
3268    * <p>
3269    * These properties are intentionally host-only:
3270    * <ul>
3271    *    <li>{@code path} / {@code paths} &mdash; top-level mount declarations only meaningful for the class registered
3272    *       with the servlet container.  A mixin sub-context inheriting the host's mount paths could cause
3273    *       nonsensical routing decisions.
3274    *    <li>{@code mixins} &mdash; the host owns the mixin discovery chain; a mixin sub-context inheriting the host's
3275    *       mixin list would loop or duplicate route registration.
3276    *    <li>{@code children} &mdash; child sub-resources are tied to the host's URL namespace, not the mixin's.
3277    * </ul>
3278    *
3279    * <p>
3280    * The host's {@link RestContext} reads {@code path}/{@code paths} from its own annotation chain only (via
3281    * {@link #getRestAnnotations()} rather than {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String)}); this allowlist
3282    * preserves that invariant when a mixin sub-context walks annotations for these properties.
3283    */
3284   private static final Set<String> HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES = Set.of(
3285      PROPERTY_path, PROPERTY_paths, PROPERTY_mixins, "children"
3286   );
3287
3288   /**
3289    * Returns the {@link Rest} annotations on the resource class hierarchy for the specified property,
3290    * in <b>parent-to-child</b> order, with {@code noInherit} cutoff applied.
3291    *
3292    * <p>
3293    * Used by both this class and {@link RestOpContext} to walk the class-level annotation chain when
3294    * computing memoized op-level settings that inherit from class-level {@code @Rest(...)} attributes.
3295    *
3296    * <p>
3297    * When this context is a {@linkplain #isMixinContext() mixin sub-context}, the parent context's annotation
3298    * chain is prepended to the mixin's own &mdash; serializers, parsers, encoders, converters, response
3299    * processors, guards, callLogger, debugEnablement, messages, and other contribution lists inherit from the
3300    * host first, with the mixin's contributions appended.  The local {@code @Rest(noInherit)} on the mixin
3301    * cuts off the parent walk for any specific property; the {@link #HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES} allowlist
3302    * unconditionally skips the parent walk for properties whose semantics are host-only (mount paths, the
3303    * mixin discovery chain, and child sub-resources).
3304    *
3305    * @param name The annotation property name (e.g. {@code "converters"}, {@code "guards"}).
3306    * @return A stream of {@link AnnotationInfo} entries in parent-to-child order, never {@code null}.
3307    */
3308   Stream<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String name) {
3309      var localAnnotations = getRestAnnotations();
3310      List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> annotations;
3311      if (isMixinContextField() && parentContext != null && !HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES.contains(name) && !noInherit.get().contains(name)) {
3312         var combined = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>(localAnnotations.size() + parentContext.getRestAnnotations().size());
3313         // Local (child-to-parent) annotations first; then parent's (child-to-parent).
3314         // Since the final stream is reversed by rstream(), the combined order becomes:
3315         //   parent's parent-to-child, then mixin's parent-to-child — i.e. host annotations come first, then mixin.
3316         combined.addAll(localAnnotations);
3317         combined.addAll(parentContext.getRestAnnotations());
3318         annotations = combined;
3319      } else {
3320         annotations = localAnnotations;
3321      }
3322      var cutoff = annotations.size();
3323      for (var i = 0; i < annotations.size(); i++) {
3324         if (resolveCdl(annotations.get(i).getStringArray(PROPERTY_noInherit)).anyMatch(name::equalsIgnoreCase)) {
3325            cutoff = i + 1;
3326            break;
3327         }
3328      }
3329      var resolved = annotations.subList(0, cutoff);
3330      // Mixin override injection (cleanup #3): for a mixin sub-context carrying host @Mixin overrides, the
3331      // synthetic override annotation is the most-derived contribution.  `resolved` is in child-to-parent order
3332      // (it is reversed to parent-to-child by rstream below), so prepending here places the override LAST in the
3333      // emitted stream — list-shaped props append it after the inherited host+mixin chain, replace-shaped props
3334      // take it as the final (winning) value.  It is added after the noInherit cutoff scan so it is never itself
3335      // cut, and HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES are excluded (path/paths/mixins/children are not @Mixin override slots).
3336      var override = HOST_ONLY_PROPERTIES.contains(name) ? null : mixinOverrideAnnotation.get();
3337      if (override == null)
3338         return rstream(resolved);
3339      var withOverride = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<Rest>>(resolved.size() + 1);
3340      withOverride.add(override);
3341      withOverride.addAll(resolved);
3342      return rstream(withOverride);
3343   }
3344
3345   /**
3346    * For a {@linkplain #isMixinContext() mixin sub-context}, returns the <b>host</b> resource class's
3347    * class-level annotation infos (host class chain, in parent-to-child order) so a mixin operation can
3348    * inherit the host's class-level {@link ContextApply @ContextApply} config
3349    * (e.g. {@link HtmlDocConfig @HtmlDocConfig},
3350    * {@link SerializerConfig @SerializerConfig}).
3351    *
3352    * <p>
3353    * Used by {@link RestOpContext.Builder} to prepend the host's class-level config annotations <i>ahead of</i>
3354    * the mixin class's own class annotations in the op's annotation work-list, so the effective precedence is
3355    * method &gt; mixin-class &gt; host-class.  This is the page-decoration counterpart of the context-level
3356    * inheritance walk in {@link #getRestAnnotationsForProperty(String)}: that walk inherits {@code @Rest}
3357    * properties (serializers, parsers, guards, ...) from the host, while this method inherits standalone
3358    * class-level config annotations that are not {@code @Rest} attributes.
3359    *
3360    * <p>
3361    * Inheritance is automatic for every mixin sub-context.  A mixin can opt out of inheriting a specific host
3362    * config annotation by naming its annotation type in {@link Rest#noInherit() @Rest(noInherit)} on the mixin
3363    * class (e.g. {@code @Rest(noInherit={"HtmlDocConfig"})}), reusing the same {@code noInherit} resolution that
3364    * gates the context-level walk so the opt-out is uniform.  For non-mixin contexts (top-level resources and
3365    * child resources) this returns an empty stream &mdash; only mixin ops inherit from a composition host.
3366    *
3367    * @param ap The annotation provider to resolve the host class chain with.
3368    * @return The host class chain's annotation infos in parent-to-child order, or an empty stream when this is
3369    *    not a mixin sub-context (or has no parent).
3370    */
3371   Stream<AnnotationInfo<?>> getInheritedHostClassAnnotations(AnnotationProvider ap) {
3372      if (! isMixinContextField() || parentContext == null)
3373         return Stream.empty();
3374      var blocked = noInherit.get();
3375      return rstream(ap.find(ClassInfo.of(parentContext.getResourceClass()), SELF, PARENTS))
3376         .filter(ai -> ! blocked.contains(ai.annotationType().getSimpleName()));
3377   }
3378
3379   /**
3380    * Returns all {@link Rest} annotations on the resource class hierarchy, in child-to-parent order.
3381    *
3382    * @return An unmodifiable list of {@link AnnotationInfo} for {@link Rest}, never {@code null}.
3383    */
3384   protected List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> getRestAnnotations() {
3385      return restAnnotations.get();
3386   }
3387
3388   /**
3389    * Returns the nearest {@link Rest} annotation on this resource.
3390    *
3391    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the first (most-derived) {@link Rest} {@link AnnotationInfo}.
3392    */
3393   protected Optional<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> getRestAnnotation() {
3394      return getRestAnnotations().stream().findFirst();
3395   }
3396
3397   /**
3398    * Returns the {@link Rest} annotations for this resource in parent-to-child (top-down) order.
3399    *
3400    * <p>
3401    * This is the reverse of {@link #getRestAnnotations()}: most-ancestor annotation first,
3402    * most-derived last. Use this when annotation values should accumulate in inheritance order
3403    * (e.g. parent values are applied first and child values append or override them).
3404    *
3405    * @return An unmodifiable list of {@link AnnotationInfo} for {@link Rest}, never {@code null}.
3406    */
3407   protected List<AnnotationInfo<Rest>> getRestAnnotationsTopDown() {
3408      return restAnnotationsTopDown.get();
3409   }
3410
3411   /**
3412    * Returns {@code true} if values for the given annotation attribute may be inherited from {@link #parentContext}.
3413    *
3414    * <p>
3415    * Inheritance is blocked when the nearest {@code @Rest(noInherit)} lists the property name (case-insensitive).
3416    *
3417    * @param property The annotation attribute name (e.g. {@code "allowedSerializerOptions"}).
3418    * @return {@code true} if parent values should be included.
3419    */
3420   protected boolean isInherited(String property) {
3421      return RestContext.this.parentContext != null && !noInherit.get().contains(property);
3422   }
3423
3424   /**
3425    * Returns {@code true} if the local {@code @Rest(noInherit=...)} list contains {@code property} as a literal token.
3426    *
3427    * <p>
3428    * Unlike the {@link #noInherit} memoizer this performs a raw, SVL-free, comma-split check on the local
3429    * {@code @Rest} annotation. It exists for callers that are themselves on the dependency chain of the
3430    * {@code varResolver} memoizer (e.g. the {@code messages} memoizer) — those callers can't reach the
3431    * regular {@link #noInherit} memoizer without inducing a cycle, because the regular path runs
3432    * {@code resolveCdl(...)} → {@link #getVarResolver()} → {@link #getMessages()} → back to messages.
3433    *
3434    * @param property The property name (e.g. {@code "messages"}). Case-insensitive comparison.
3435    * @return {@code true} if {@code property} is named as a literal token in the local {@code @Rest(noInherit=...)}.
3436    */
3437   private boolean isNoInheritLiteral(String property) {
3438      var fromRest = getRestAnnotation()
3439         .map(x -> x.getStringArray("noInherit").orElse(StringUtils.EMPTY_STRING_ARRAY))
3440         .stream()
3441         .flatMap(Arrays::stream)
3442         .filter(Objects::nonNull)
3443         .flatMap(s -> StringUtils.split(s, ',').stream())
3444         .map(String::trim)
3445         .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank)
3446         .anyMatch(property::equalsIgnoreCase);
3447      if (fromRest)
3448         return true;
3449      // Also honor a host @Mixin(noInherit=...) token (raw / SVL-free, same as the @Rest path above) so the
3450      // messages memoizer's cutoff check sees both noInherit sources without inducing the resolver cycle.
3451      if (resolvedMixin != null) {
3452         for (var s : resolvedMixin.overrides().noInherit())
3453            for (var t : StringUtils.split(s, ','))
3454               if (property.equalsIgnoreCase(t.trim()))
3455                  return true;
3456      }
3457      return false;
3458   }
3459
3460   /**
3461    * Resolves comma-delimited annotation values with SVL variable substitution.
3462    *
3463    * @param values Raw annotation attribute values.
3464    * @return A stream of trimmed, non-blank tokens.
3465    */
3466   private Stream<String> resolveCdl(String...values) {
3467      if (values == null || values.length == 0)
3468         return Stream.empty();
3469      return Arrays.stream(values)
3470         .filter(Objects::nonNull)
3471         .map(this::resolve)
3472         .map(StringUtils::split)
3473         .flatMap(Collection::stream)
3474         .map(String::trim)
3475         .filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank);
3476   }
3477
3478   private Stream<String> resolveCdl(Optional<String[]> values) {
3479      return values.isEmpty() ? Stream.empty() : resolveCdl(values.get());
3480   }
3481
3482   /**
3483    * Resolves SVL variables in the given string.
3484    *
3485    * @param s The raw string. Can be {@code null}.
3486    * @return The resolved string.
3487    */
3488   protected String resolve(String s) {
3489      return getVarResolver().resolve(s);
3490   }
3491
3492   /**
3493    * Returns the parser session-option keys allowed for this resource.
3494    *
3495    * @return An unmodifiable case-insensitive sorted set, never {@code null}.
3496    */
3497   public SortedSet<String> getAllowedParserOptions() {
3498      return allowedParserOptions.get();
3499   }
3500
3501   /**
3502    * Returns the serializer session-option keys allowed for this resource.
3503    *
3504    * @return An unmodifiable case-insensitive sorted set, never {@code null}.
3505    */
3506   public SortedSet<String> getAllowedSerializerOptions() {
3507      return allowedSerializerOptions.get();
3508   }
3509
3510   /**
3511    * Called during servlet destruction to invoke all {@link RestDestroy} methods.
3512    *
3513    * <p>
3514    * Fires this context's own {@code @RestDestroy} methods first, then walks any mixin sub-contexts
3515    * (host destroys before each mixin's), then any {@code @Rest(children=...)} sub-resources, then closes
3516    * the bean store. Mixin sub-contexts recursively destroy themselves via the same path; flat-inheritance
3517    * guarantees no further mixin descent occurs from inside a mixin sub-context.
3518    */
3519   public void destroy() {
3520      for (var x : destroyInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
3521         try {
3522            x.invoke(beanStore, getResource());
3523         } catch (Exception e) {
3524            getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, unwrap(e), () -> String.format("Error occurred invoking servlet-destroy method '%s'.", x.getFullName()));
3525         }
3526      }
3527      if (!isMixinContext) {
3528         for (var mctx : mixinContexts.get().values()) {
3529            try {
3530               mctx.destroy();
3531            } catch (Exception e) {
3532               getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, unwrap(e), () -> String.format("Error occurred destroying mixin sub-context '%s'.", mctx.getResourceClass().getName()));
3533            }
3534         }
3535      }
3536      var childrenRef = getRestChildren();
3537      if (nn(childrenRef))
3538         childrenRef.destroy();
3539      try {
3540         beanStore.close();
3541      } catch (Exception e) {
3542         getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, unwrap(e), () -> "Error occurred closing bean store.");
3543      }
3544   }
3545
3546   /**
3547    * The main service method.
3548    *
3549    * <p>
3550    * Subclasses can optionally override this method if they want to tailor the behavior of requests.
3551    *
3552    * @param resource
3553    *    The REST servlet or bean that this context defines.
3554    *    <br>Note that this bean may not be the same bean used during initialization as it may have been replaced at runtime.
3555    * @param r1 The incoming HTTP servlet request object.
3556    * @param r2 The incoming HTTP servlet response object.
3557    * @throws ServletException General servlet exception.
3558    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
3559    */
3560   @SuppressWarnings({
3561      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for request execution logic
3562   })
3563   public void execute(Object resource, HttpServletRequest r1, HttpServletResponse r2) throws ServletException, IOException {
3564
3565      // Must be careful not to bleed thread-locals.
3566      if (nn(localSession.get()))
3567         LOG.warning("WARNING:  Thread-local call object was not cleaned up from previous request.  {}, thread=[{}]", this, Thread.currentThread().getName());
3568
3569      RestSession.Builder sb = createSession().resource(resource).req(r1).res(r2).logger(getCallLogger());
3570
3571      try {
3572
3573         if (nn(initException))
3574            throw initException;
3575
3576         // If the resource path contains variables (e.g. @Rest(path="/f/{a}/{b}"), then we want to resolve
3577         // those variables and push the servletPath to include the resolved variables.  The new pathInfo will be
3578         // the remainder after the new servletPath.
3579         // Only do this for the top-level resource because the logic for child resources are processed next.
3580         if (pathMatcher.hasVars() && parentContext == null) {
3581            var sp = sb.req().getServletPath();
3582            var pi = sb.getPathInfoUndecoded();
3583            var upi2 = UrlPath.of(pi == null ? sp : sp + pi);
3584            var uppm = pathMatcher.match(upi2);
3585         if (nn(uppm) && ! uppm.hasEmptyVars()) {
3586            sb.pathVars(uppm.getVars());
3587            var pathInfo = o(validatePathInfo(nie(urlDecode(uppm.getSuffix())))).orElse("\u0000");
3588            var servletPath = validateServletPath(uppm.getPrefix());
3589            sb.req(new HttpServletRequestWrapper(sb.req()) {
3590               @Override
3591               public String getPathInfo() {
3592                  return pathInfo.charAt(0) == (char)0 ? null : pathInfo;
3593               }
3594               @Override
3595               public String getServletPath() {
3596                  return servletPath;
3597               }
3598            });
3599         } else {
3600               var call = sb.build();
3601               call.debug(isDebug(call)).status(SC_NOT_FOUND).finish();
3602               return;
3603            }
3604         }
3605
3606         // If this resource has child resources, try to recursively call them.
3607         var childMatch = getRestChildren().findMatch(sb);
3608         if (childMatch.isPresent()) {
3609            var uppm = childMatch.get().getPathMatch();
3610            var rc = childMatch.get().getChildContext();
3611            if (! uppm.hasEmptyVars()) {
3612               sb.pathVars(uppm.getVars());
3613               var pathInfo = o(validatePathInfo(nie(urlDecode(uppm.getSuffix())))).orElse("\u0000");
3614               var servletPath = validateServletPath(sb.req().getServletPath() + uppm.getPrefix());
3615               var childRequest = new HttpServletRequestWrapper(sb.req()) {
3616                  @Override
3617                  public String getPathInfo() {
3618                     return pathInfo.charAt(0) == (char)0 ? null : pathInfo;
3619                  }
3620                  @Override
3621                  public String getServletPath() {
3622                     return servletPath;
3623                  }
3624               };
3625               rc.execute(rc.getResource(), childRequest, sb.res());
3626            } else {
3627               var call = sb.build();
3628               call.debug(isDebug(call)).status(SC_NOT_FOUND).finish();
3629            }
3630            return;
3631         }
3632
3633      } catch (Exception e) {
3634         handleError(sb.build(), convertThrowable(e));
3635      }
3636
3637      var s = sb.build();
3638
3639      try {
3640         localSession.set(s);
3641         s.debug(isDebug(s));
3642         startCall(s);
3643         s.run();
3644      } catch (Exception e) {
3645         handleError(s, convertThrowable(e));
3646      } finally {
3647         try {
3648            s.finish();
3649            endCall(s);
3650            // For mixin endpoints, dual-fire endCall on the mixin sub-context after the host's so host
3651            // hooks fire first, then the mixin's. Skip when no operation was resolved (e.g. 404 paths).
3652            var os = s.getOpSessionOrNull();
3653            if (os != null) {
3654               var opRestContext = os.getContext().getContext();
3655               if (opRestContext.isMixinContext)
3656                  opRestContext.endCall(s);
3657            }
3658         } finally {
3659            localSession.remove();
3660         }
3661      }
3662   }
3663

3747    * on the resource class, by registering a {@link CallLogger} bean in the bean store, or by declaring a
3748    * {@link Bean @Bean}-annotated static method on the resource class:
3749    * <p class='bjava'>
3750    *    <ja>@Bean</ja> <jk>public static</jk> CallLogger myCallLogger(<i>&lt;args&gt;</i>) {...}
