3966   /**
3967    * Returns the resolved top-level mount paths for this resource.
3968    *
3969    * <p>
3970    * Computed once during {@link RestContext} construction by walking the runtime-override resolution chain
3971    * (highest precedence first):
3972    * <ol>
3973    *    <li><b>Programmatic</b> &mdash; {@link Builder#paths(String...)} setter (or {@link Args#paths()} record
3974    *       field). Highest precedence; an empty array (not {@code null}) explicitly clears the mount list.
3975    *    <li><b>Getter</b> &mdash; the resource's {@code getPaths()} virtual method (defined on
3976    *       {@code RestServlet} and {@code RestResource}). {@code null} return inherits; non-{@code null} (including
3977    *       empty array) wins over the annotation default.
3978    *    <li><b>Annotation default</b> &mdash; {@link Rest#paths()}, walked top-down across the resource-class
3979    *       hierarchy with the most-derived non-empty value winning. Each element is
3980    *       <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL</a>-resolved
3981    *       against the bootstrap {@link VarResolver VarResolver}, then comma-split (trim
3982    *       each piece, drop empties).  A single element like {@code "$C{health.paths}"} can therefore expand
3983    *       to multiple mount paths.
3984    * </ol>
3985    *
3986    * <p>
3987    * The resolved array is what hosting runtimes &mdash; Jetty's auto-discovery flow in
3988    * {@code JettyServerComponent}, and Spring Boot's {@code ServletRegistrationBean} wiring &mdash; should
3989    * consume when mounting the resource at the top level.
3990    *
3991    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3992    *    <li class='note'>
3993    *       Returns an empty array (not {@code null}) when none of the rungs resolve. Hosting runtimes interpret
3994    *       an empty array as &quot;no top-level mounts&quot; and surface a clear error naming the resource.
3995    *    <li class='note'>
3996    *       The scalar {@link #getPath()} accessor remains orthogonal to this method.
3997    * </ul>
3998    *
3999    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4000    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#paths()}
4001    * </ul>
4002    *
4003    * @return The resolved mount paths (never {@code null}; possibly empty).
4004    * @since 10.0.0
4005    */
4006   public String[] getPaths() { return paths; }
4007
4008   /**
4009    * Returns the path matcher for this context.
4010    *
4011    * @return The path matcher for this context.
4012    */
4013   public UrlPathMatcher getPathMatcher() { return pathMatcher; }
4014
4015   /**
4016    * Returns the explicit list of supported accept types for this resource.
4017    *
4018    * <p>
4019    * Consists of the media types for production common to all operations on this class.
4020    *
4021    * <p>
4022    * Derived from {@link Rest#produces()} across the resource annotation chain.
4023    *
4024    * @return
4025    *    An unmodifiable list of supported <c>Accept</c> header values for this resource.
4026    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4027    */
4028   public List<MediaType> getProduces() { return produces.get(); }
4029
4030   /**
4031    * Returns the resource object.
4032    *
4033    * <p>
4034    * This is the instance of the class annotated with the {@link Rest @Rest} annotation, usually
4035    * an instance of {@link RestServlet}.
4036    *
4037    * @return
4038    *    The resource object.
4039    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4040    */
4041   public Object getResource() { return resource.get(); }
4042
4043   /**
4044    * Returns the resource class type.
4045    *
4046    * @return The resource class type.
4047    */
4048   public Class<?> getResourceClass() { return resourceClass; }
4049
4050   /**
4051    * Returns the response processors registered on this resource.
4052    *
4053    * <p>
4054    * Returned in the order they're invoked by {@link #processResponse(RestOpSession)}.
4055    * The returned array is the live backing array — callers must not mutate it.
4056    *
4057    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4058    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#responseProcessors}
4059    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ResponseProcessors">Response Processors</a>
4060    * </ul>
4061    *
4062    * @return
4063    *    The response processors for this resource.
4064    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4065    */
4066   public ResponseProcessor[] getResponseProcessors() { return beanStore.getBean(ResponseProcessor[].class).orElse(null); }
4067
4068   /**
4069    * Returns the {@link RestOpArg} classes registered on this resource.
4070    *
4071    * <p>
4072    * Per-op {@link RestOpArg} instances are resolved separately at per-op setup via the bean store
4073    * (see {@link #findRestOperationArgs(Method, BeanStore)}); this getter returns the class list
4074    * that drives that resolution.
4075    *
4076    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4077    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#restOpArgs}
4078    * </ul>
4079    *
4080    * @return
4081    *    The REST-op-arg classes for this resource.
4082    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4083    */
4084   public Class<? extends RestOpArg>[] getRestOpArgs() { return restOpArgs.get(); }
4085
4086   /**
4087    * Returns the child resources associated with this servlet.
4088    *
4089    * @return
4090    *    An unmodifiable map of child resources.
4091    *    Keys are the {@link Rest#path() @Rest(path)} annotation defined on the child resource.
4092    */
4093   public RestChildren getRestChildren() { return beanStore.getBean(RestChildren.class).orElse(null); }
4094
4095   /**
4096    * Returns the REST Java methods defined in this resource.
4097    *
4098    * <p>
4099    * These are the methods annotated with the {@link RestOp @RestOp} annotation.
4100    *
4101    * @return
4102    *    An unmodifiable map of Java method names to call method objects.
4103    */
4104   public RestOperations getRestOperations() { return beanStore.getBean(RestOperations.class).orElse(null); }
4105
4106   /**
4107    * Returns the bootstrap bean store for this context.
4108    *
4109    * <p>
4110    * This is the bean store inherited from the parent resource and does not include
4111    * any beans added by this class.
4112    *
4113    * @return The bootstrap bean store for this context.
4114    */
4115   public WritableBeanStore getBootstrapBeanStore() { return bootstrapBeanStore; }
4116
4117   /**
4118    * Returns the serializers associated with this context.
4119    *
4120    * @return The serializers associated with this context.
4121    */
4122   public SerializerSet getSerializers() { return beanStore.getBean(SerializerSet.class).orElse(null); }
4123
4124   /**
4125    * Returns the servlet init parameter returned by {@link ServletConfig#getInitParameter(String)}.
4126    *
4127    * @param name The init parameter name.
4128    * @return The servlet init parameter, or <jk>null</jk> if not found.
4129    */
4130   public String getServletInitParameter(String name) {
4131      return builder.getInitParameter(name);
4132   }
4133
4134   /**
4135    * Returns the static files associated with this context.
4136    *
4137    * @return
4138    *    The static files for this resource.
4139    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4140    */
4141   public StaticFiles getStaticFiles() { return beanStore.getBean(StaticFiles.class).orElse(null); }
4142
4143   /**
4144    * Gives access to the internal statistics on this context.
4145    *
4146    * @return The context statistics.
4147    */
4148   public RestContextStats getStats() { return new RestContextStats(startTime, getMethodExecStore().getStatsByTotalTime()); }
4149
4150   /**
4151    * Returns the swagger for the REST resource.
4152    *
4153    * @param locale The locale of the swagger to return.
4154    * @return The swagger as an {@link Optional}.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
4155    */
4156   public Optional<Swagger> getSwagger(Locale locale) {
4157      Swagger s = swaggerCache.get(locale);
4158      if (s == null) {
4159         try {
4160            var provider = getSwaggerProvider();
4161            if (provider == null)
4162               return oe();
4163            s = provider.getSwagger(this, locale);
4164            if (nn(s))
4165               swaggerCache.put(locale, s);
4166         } catch (Exception e) {
4167            throw new InternalServerError(e);
4168         }
4169      }
4170      return o(s);
4171   }
4172
4173   /**
4174    * Returns the Swagger provider used by this resource.
4175    *
4176    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4177    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#swaggerProvider()}
4178    * </ul>
4179    *
4180    * @return
4181    *    The information provider for this resource.
4182    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4183    */
4184   public SwaggerProvider getSwaggerProvider() { return beanStore.getBean(SwaggerProvider.class).orElse(null); }
4185
4186   /**
4187    * Returns the OpenAPI 3.1 document for the REST resource.
4188    *
4189    * @param locale The locale of the document to return.
4190    * @return The OpenAPI document as an {@link Optional}.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
4191    */
4192   public Optional<OpenApi> getOpenApi(Locale locale) {
4193      OpenApi o = openApiCache.get(locale);
4194      if (o == null) {
4195         try {
4196            var provider = getOpenApiProvider();
4197            if (provider == null)
4198               return oe();
4199            o = provider.getOpenApi(this, locale);
4200            if (nn(o))
4201               openApiCache.put(locale, o);
4202         } catch (Exception e) {
4203            throw new InternalServerError(e);
4204         }
4205      }
4206      return o(o);
4207   }
4208
4209   /**
4210    * Returns the OpenAPI 3.1 provider used by this resource.
4211    *
4212    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4213    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#openApiProvider()}
4214    * </ul>
4215    *
4216    * @return
4217    *    The OpenAPI 3.1 provider for this resource.
4218    *    <br>May be <jk>null</jk>.
4219    */
4220   public OpenApiProvider getOpenApiProvider() { return beanStore.getBean(OpenApiProvider.class).orElse(null); }
4221
4222   /**
4223    * Returns the stack trace database associated with this context.
4224    *
4225    * @return
4226    *    The stack trace database for this resource.
4227    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4228    */
4229   public ThrownStore getThrownStore() { return beanStore.getBean(ThrownStore.class).orElse(null); }
4230
4231   /**
4232    * Returns the authority path of the resource.
4233    *
4234    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4235    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#uriAuthority()}
4236    * </ul>
4237    *
4238    * @return
4239    *    The authority path of this resource.
4240    *    <br>If not specified, returns the context path of the ascendant resource.
4241    */
4242   public String getUriAuthority() {
4243      return uriAuthority.get();
4244   }
4245
4246   /**
4247    * Returns the context path of the resource.
4248    *
4249    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4250    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#uriContext()}
4251    * </ul>
4252    *
4253    * @return
4254    *    The context path of this resource.
4255    *    <br>If not specified, returns the context path of the ascendant resource.
4256    */
4257   public String getUriContext() {
4258      return uriContext.get();
4259   }
4260
4261   /**
4262    * Returns the setting on how relative URIs should be interpreted as relative to.
4263    *
4264    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4265    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#uriRelativity()}
4266    * </ul>
4267    *
4268    * @return
4269    *    The URI-resolution relativity setting value.
4270    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4271    */
4272   public UriRelativity getUriRelativity() { return uriRelativity.get(); }
4273
4274   /**
4275    * Returns the setting on how relative URIs should be resolved.
4276    *
4277    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4278    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#uriResolution()}
4279    * </ul>
4280    *
4281    * @return
4282    *    The URI-resolution setting value.
4283    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
4284    */
4285   public UriResolution getUriResolution() { return uriResolution.get(); }
4286
4287   /**
4288    * Returns the variable resolver for this servlet.
4289    *
4290    * <p>
4291    * Variable resolvers are used to replace variables in property values.
4292    * They can be nested arbitrarily deep.
4293    * They can also return values that themselves contain other variables.
4294    *
4295    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
4296    * <p class='bjava'>
4297    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
4298    *       messages=<js>"nls/Messages"</js>,
4299    *       properties={
4300    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"title"</js>,value=<js>"$L{title}"</js>),  <jc>// Localized variable in Messages.properties</jc>
4301    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"javaVendor"</js>,value=<js>"$S{java.vendor,Oracle}"</js>),  <jc>// System property with default value</jc>
4302    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"foo"</js>,value=<js>"bar"</js>),
4303    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"bar"</js>,value=<js>"baz"</js>),
4304    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"v1"</js>,value=<js>"$R{foo}"</js>),  <jc>// Request variable.  value="bar"</jc>
4305    *          <ja>@Property</ja>(name=<js>"v1"</js>,value=<js>"$R{foo,bar}"</js>),  <jc>// Request variable.  value="bar"</jc>
4306    *       }
4307    *    )
4308    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyRestResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
4309    * </p>
4310    *
4311    * <p>
4312    * A typical usage pattern involves using variables inside the {@link HtmlDocConfig @HtmlDocConfig} annotation:
4313    * <p class='bjava'>
4314    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/{name}/*"</js>)
4315    *    <ja>@HtmlDocConfig</ja>(
4316    *       navlinks={
4317    *          <js>"up: $R{requestParentURI}"</js>,
4318    *          <js>"api: servlet:/api"</js>,
4319    *          <js>"stats: servlet:/stats"</js>,
4320    *          <js>"editLevel: servlet:/editLevel?logger=$A{attribute.name, OFF}"</js>
4321    *       }
4322    *       header={
4323    *          <js>"&lt;h1&gt;$L{MyLocalizedPageTitle}&lt;/h1&gt;"</js>
4324    *       },
4325    *       aside={
4326    *          <js>"$F{resources/AsideText.html}"</js>
4327    *       }
4328    *    )
4329    *    <jk>public</jk> LoggerEntry getLogger(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>, <ja>@Path</ja> String <jv>name</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
4330    * </p>
4331    *
4332    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4333    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
4334    * </ul>
4335    *
4336    * @return The var resolver in use by this resource.
4337    */
4338   public VarResolver getVarResolver() { return beanStore.getBean(VarResolver.class).orElse(null); }
4339
4340   /**
4341    * Returns the bootstrap (pre-runtime) variable resolver used during context construction.
4342    *
4343    * <p>
4344    * The bootstrap resolver has the same {@link Var} catalog as {@link #getVarResolver()} but does not have
4345    * {@link Messages} or {@link Config} beans wired in — it is used to resolve annotation attribute values
4346    * (e.g. <c>@Rest(messages=...)</c>) before those beans are built. Override via
4347    * {@link Bean @Bean(name="bootstrapVarResolver")} on a static method of the resource class.
4348    *
4349    * @return The bootstrap var resolver in use by this resource.
4350    */
4351   public VarResolver getBootstrapVarResolver() { return bootstrapVarResolver.get(); }
4352
4353   /**
4354    * Returns whether it's safe to pass the HTTP content as a <js>"content"</js> GET parameter.
4355    *
4356    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4357    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#disableContentParam()}
4358    * </ul>
4359    *
4360    * @return <jk>true</jk> if setting is enabled.
4361    */
4362   public boolean isAllowContentParam() { return allowContentParam.get(); }
4363
4364   /**
4365    * Returns whether it's safe to render stack traces in HTTP responses.
4366    *
4367    * @return <jk>true</jk> if setting is enabled.
4368    */
4369   public boolean isRenderResponseStackTraces() { return renderResponseStackTraces.get(); }
4370
4371   /**
4372    * Returns whether this resource is opted into
4373    * <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807">RFC 7807</a>
4374    * {@code application/problem+json} error responses.
4375    *
4376    * <p>
4377    * When {@code true}:
4378    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
4379    *    <li>Thrown {@code BasicHttpException}s are serialized as {@code application/problem+json} via
4380    *       {@code RestContext.handleError} &mdash; regardless of the client's {@code Accept} header.
4381    *    <li>{@code @RestOp} methods that return a {@link Problem} (or throw a
4382    *       {@link ProblemException}) are serialized by
4383    *       {@link ProblemDetailsProcessor}, but only when the client's
4384    *       {@code Accept} header matches {@code application/problem+json} (or {@code *&#47;*}).
4385    * </ul>
4386    *
4387    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4388    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#problemDetails()}
4389    * </ul>
4390    *
4391    * @return <jk>true</jk> if RFC 7807 problem-details responses are enabled on this resource.
4392    */
4393   public boolean isProblemDetails() { return problemDetails.get(); }
4394
4395   /**
4396    * Returns whether the resource opted into per-request virtual-thread dispatch (Java 21+) via
4397    * {@code @Rest(virtualThreads=true)}.
4398    *
4399    * <p>
4400    * The flag is honored only when {@link #getVirtualThreadExecutor()} returns a non-{@code null} executor; on
4401    * runtimes older than Java 21 the executor is {@code null} and the flag is logged once at context init.
4402    *
4403    * @return <jk>true</jk> if virtual-thread dispatch is configured on this resource.
4404    */
4405   public boolean isVirtualThreadsEnabled() { return virtualThreadsEnabled.get(); }
4406
4407   /**
4408    * Returns the raw resource-level observability attribute value from {@code @Rest(observability)}.
4409    *
4410    * <ul class='values'>
4411    *    <li>{@code "true"} &mdash; strict opt-in: all operations on this resource require a wired backend.
4412    *    <li>{@code "false"} &mdash; explicit opt-out: the observability block is disabled for all operations.
4413    *    <li>{@code null} / empty &mdash; default (inherit / no-op fallback) behavior.
4414    * </ul>
4415    *
4416    * <p>
4417    * Per-operation overrides from {@link RestOp#observability()} (and verb
4418    * annotations) take precedence over this value; they are resolved in {@link RestOpContext}.
4419    *
4420    * @return The resolved {@code @Rest(observability)} string, or {@code null} when not set.
4421    */
4422   public String getObservabilityAttribute() { return observabilityAttribute.get(); }
4423
4424   /**
4425    * Returns whether the resource explicitly opted out of observability via {@code @Rest(observability="false")}.
4426    *
4427    * @return <jk>true</jk> when the resource-level observability is disabled.
4428    */
4429   public boolean isObservabilityDisabled() {
4430      var v = observabilityAttribute.get();
4431      return v != null && v.equalsIgnoreCase("false");
4432   }
4433
4434   /**
4435    * Returns whether the server writes W3C {@code traceparent} / {@code tracestate} response headers when a
4436    * {@link TracerHook} is active on the request.
4437    *
4438    * <p>
4439    * Defaults to {@code true} (on-when-tracer); resolved from the {@code RestContext.responseTraceparent}
4440    * env-driven default. When {@code true}, a {@link TraceContextResponseProcessor} is registered in the
4441    * response-processor chain &mdash; it writes the headers only when a non-no-op {@code TracerHook} stashed a
4442    * trace context for the request, so it stays zero-cost on the no-tracer path. When {@code false}, the
4443    * processor is not registered at all.
4444    *
4445    * @return <jk>true</jk> if trace-context response-header propagation is enabled on this resource.
4446    */
4447   public boolean isResponseTraceparent() { return defaultResponseTraceparent; }
4448
4449   /**
4450    * Returns whether SLF4J MDC propagation is enabled for {@link CompletableFuture}
4451    * completion threads on this resource.
4452    *
4453    * <p>
4454    * Defaults to {@code true}; resolved first from the programmatic {@link Builder#mdcAsyncPropagation(boolean)}
4455    * override (if set), then from the {@code RestContext.mdcAsyncPropagation} env-driven default.
4456    * When {@code true}, {@link MdcAsyncListener} wraps the
4457    * {@link CompletionStage#whenComplete(BiConsumer) whenComplete} callback
4458    * registered by {@link AsyncResponseProcessor} so the completion thread sees
4459    * the request thread's MDC context. When {@code false}, no MDC work is done.
4460    *
4461    * @return <jk>true</jk> if MDC async propagation is enabled on this resource.
4462    */
4463   public boolean isMdcAsyncPropagation() {
4464      return builder.mdcAsyncPropagation != null ? builder.mdcAsyncPropagation : defaultMdcAsyncPropagation;
4465   }
4466
4467   /**
4468    * Returns the lazily-instantiated virtual-thread executor for this resource, or {@code null} when
4469    * {@code @Rest(virtualThreads=true)} is unset, the runtime is older than Java 21, or executor construction
4470    * failed (in which case a {@code WARNING} was logged at context init).
4471    *
4472    * @return The executor, or {@code null} if virtual-thread dispatch is not active for this resource.
4473    */
4474   public Executor getVirtualThreadExecutor() { return virtualThreadExecutor.get(); }
4475
4476   /**
4477    * Returns the configured async-response timeout (milliseconds) for this resource. {@code -1} indicates that no
4478    * value was supplied at the resource level — the per-op value (or {@code AsyncResponseProcessor}'s 30-second
4479    * default) applies in that case.
4480    *
4481    * @return The async timeout in milliseconds, or {@code -1} when unset.
4482    */
4483   public long getAsyncTimeoutMillis() { return asyncTimeoutMillis.get(); }
4484
4485   /**
4486    * Returns the {@link Executor} configured for routing
4487    * {@link CompletableFuture} completion callbacks on this resource, or
4488    * {@code null} when {@code @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)} is unset (natural completion thread).
4489    *
4490    * @return The executor, or {@code null} if no async-completion executor is configured.
4491    * @since 10.0.0
4492    */
4493   public Executor getAsyncCompletionExecutor() { return asyncCompletionExecutor.get(); }
4494
4495   /**
4496    * Returns whether framework beans and operation/child contexts are eagerly initialized at construction time.
4497    *
4498    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4499    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#eagerInit()}
4500    * </ul>
4501    *
4502    * @return <jk>true</jk> if eager initialization is enabled.
4503    */
4504   public boolean isEagerInit() { return eagerInit.get(); }
4505
4506   /**
4507    * Returns whether this resource's {@code @Rest(children=...)} entries are built lazily on first invocation
4508    * rather than eagerly at parent startup.
4509    *
4510    * <p>
4511    * Resolution order (highest wins):
4512    * <ol>
4513    *   <li>{@link Builder#lazyChildInit(boolean)} programmatic knob (if explicitly set).</li>
4514    *   <li>{@link Rest#lazyChildren() @Rest(lazyChildren)} annotation chain (most-derived wins).</li>
4515    *   <li>{@code RestContext.lazyChildren} env-driven default (default {@code false}).</li>
4516    * </ol>
4517    *
4518    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4519    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#lazyChildren()}
4520    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#lazyChildInit(boolean)}
4521    * </ul>
4522    *
4523    * @return <jk>true</jk> if lazy child initialization is enabled.
4524    * @since 10.0.0
4525    */
4526   public boolean isLazyChildren() {
4527      return builder.lazyChildInit != null ? builder.lazyChildInit : lazyChildrenAnnotation.get();
4528   }
4529
4530   /**
4531    * Called during servlet initialization to invoke all {@link RestPostInit} child-last methods.
4532    *
4533    * @return This object.
4534    * @throws ServletException Error occurred.
4535    */
4536   public synchronized RestContext postInit() throws ServletException {
4537      if (initialized.get())
4538         return this;
4539      var resource2 = getResource();
4540      initializeResourceContext(resource2);
4541      for (var x : postInitInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
4542         try {
4543            x.invoke(beanStore, getResource());
4544         } catch (Exception e) {
4545            throw new ServletException(unwrap(e));
4546         }
4547      }
4548      getRestChildren().postInit();
4549      return this;
4550   }
4551
4552   private void initializeResourceContext(Object resource2) {
4553      var mi = ClassInfo.of(resource2).getMethod(x -> x.hasName("setContext") && x.hasParameterTypes(RestContext.class)).orElse(null);
4554      if (nn(mi)) {
4555         try {
4556            mi.accessible().invoke(resource2, this);
4557         } catch (ExecutableException e) {
4558            throw new RuntimeException(e.unwrap());
4559         }
4560      }
4561   }
4562
4563   /**
4564    * Called during servlet initialization to invoke all {@link RestPostInit} child-first methods.
4565    *
4566    * @return This object.
4567    * @throws ServletException Error occurred.
4568    */
4569   public RestContext postInitChildFirst() throws ServletException {
4570      if (initialized.get())
4571         return this;
4572      getRestChildren().postInitChildFirst();
4573      for (var x : postInitChildFirstInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
4574         try {
4575            x.invoke(beanStore, getResource());
4576         } catch (Exception e) {
4577            throw new ServletException(unwrap(e));
4578         }
4579      }
4580      initialized.set(true);
4581      return this;
4582   }
4583
4584   private boolean isDebug(RestSession call) {
4585      return getDebugConfig().resolve(this, call.getRequest()).enabled();
4586   }
4587
4588   /**
4589    * Validates that when {@code @Rest(observability="true")} is declared on this resource, at least one real
4590    * observability backend ({@link MetricsRecorder} or
4591    * {@link TracerHook}) is registered in the bean store.
4592    *
4593    * <p>
4594    * Called once during {@link RestContext} construction, after all {@code @Bean} method injection has run.
4595    * If the attribute is {@code "true"} and both beans resolve to their {@code NoOp} singletons, construction
4596    * fails with a {@link BasicHttpException InternalServerError} whose message precisely
4597    * identifies the missing backends.
4598    *
4599    * <p>
4600    * Per-operation {@code @RestOp(observability="true")} startup-fails are handled the same way inside
4601    * {@link RestOpContext}.
4602    *
4603    * @throws InternalServerError when {@code @Rest(observability="true")} is set but no backend is wired.
4604    */
4605   private void checkObservabilityBackendPresent() {
4606      var attr = observabilityAttribute.get();
4607      if (!"true".equalsIgnoreCase(attr))
4608         return;
4609      var recorder = beanStore.getBean(MetricsRecorder.class).orElse(null);
4610      var tracer = beanStore.getBean(TracerHook.class).orElse(null);
4611      boolean hasRecorder = recorder != null && !(recorder instanceof NoOpMetricsRecorder);
4612      boolean hasTracer = tracer != null && !(tracer instanceof NoOpTracerHook);
4613      if (!hasRecorder && !hasTracer)
4614         throw new InternalServerError(
4615            "@Rest(observability=\"true\") is set on " + getResourceClass().getSimpleName()
4616            + " but no MetricsRecorder or TracerHook @Bean is registered. "
4617            + "Either wire a backend or change observability to \"\" (inherit) or \"false\" (opt-out).");
4618   }
4619
4620   /**
4621    * Eagerly evaluates the {@link #asyncCompletionExecutor} memoizer when the annotation or programmatic
4622    * override is non-blank, ensuring "bean not found" surfaces as a startup failure.
4623    *
4624    * @throws IllegalStateException if the named executor bean is not in the bean store.
4625    */
4626   private void checkAsyncCompletionExecutorPresent() {
4627      var name = builder.asyncCompletionExecutorName != null
4628         ? builder.asyncCompletionExecutorName
4629         : mergeReplacedStringAttribute(PROPERTY_asyncCompletionExecutor, null);
4630      if (name != null && !name.isBlank())
4631         asyncCompletionExecutor.get(); // throws IllegalStateException if bean is missing
4632   }
4633
4634   private void initializeFrameworkBeansForRestOps() {
4635      getMarshallingContext();
4636      getEncoders();
4637      getSerializers();
4638      getParsers();
4639      getLogger();
4640      getThrownStore();
4641      getMethodExecStore();
4642      getMessages();
4643      getVarResolver();
4644      getConfig();
4645      getResponseProcessors();
4646      getCallLogger();
4647      getPartSerializer();
4648      getPartParser();
4649      getJsonSchemaGenerator();
4650      getStaticFiles();
4651      getDefaultRequestHeaders();
4652      getDefaultResponseHeaders();
4653      getDefaultRequestAttributes();
4654      getDebugEnablement();
4655      getDebugConfig();
4656      getSwaggerProvider();
4657      getOpenApiProvider();
4658   }
4659
4660   private static Set<String> newCaseInsensitiveSet(String value) {
4661      var s = new TreeSet<>(String.CASE_INSENSITIVE_ORDER) {
4662         private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
4663
4664         @Override
4665         public boolean contains(Object v) {
4666            return v != null && super.contains(v);
4667         }
4668      };
4669      StringUtils.split(value, s::add);
4670      return u(s);
4671   }
4672
4673   private LifecycleInvokerPair buildLifecycleInvokerPair(Supplier<MethodList> methods) {
4674      var ml = MethodList.of(methods.get());
4675      var inv = ml.stream().map(this::toMethodInvoker).toArray(MethodInvoker[]::new);
4676      return new LifecycleInvokerPair(ml, inv);
4677   }
4678
4679   private MethodInvoker toMethodInvoker(Method m) {
4680      return new MethodInvoker(m, getMethodExecStats(m));
4681   }
4682
4683   private static Throwable unwrap(Throwable t) {
4684      if (t instanceof InvocationTargetException t2)
4685         return t2.getTargetException();
4686      return t;
4687   }
4688
4689   /**
4690    * Method that can be subclassed to allow uncaught throwables to be treated as other types of throwables.
4691    *
4692    * <p>
4693    * The default implementation looks at the throwable class name to determine whether it can be converted to another type:
4694    *
4695    * <ul>
4696    *    <li><js>"*AccessDenied*"</js> - Converted to {@link Unauthorized}.
4697    *    <li><js>"*Empty*"</js>,<js>"*NotFound*"</js> - Converted to {@link NotFound}.
4698    * </ul>
4699    *
4700    * @param t The thrown object.
4701    * @return The converted thrown object.
4702    */
4703   public Throwable convertThrowable(Throwable t) {
4704
4705      if (t instanceof InvocationTargetException t2)
4706         t = t2.getTargetException();
4707
4708      if (t instanceof ExecutableException t2)
4709         t = t2.getTargetException();
4710
4711      if (t instanceof BasicHttpException t2)
4712         return t2;
4713
4714      var ci = ClassInfo.of(t);
4715
4716      if (ci.hasAnnotation(Response.class))
4717         return t;
4718
4719      if (ci.isAssignableTo(ParseException.class) || ci.is(InvalidDataConversionException.class))
4720         return new BadRequest(t);
4721
4722      String n = cn(t);
4723
4724      if (co(n, "AccessDenied") || co(n, "Unauthorized"))
4725         return new Unauthorized(t);
4726
4727      if (co(n, "Empty") || co(n, "NotFound"))
4728         return new NotFound(t);
4729
4730      return t;
4731   }
4732
4733   /**
4734    * Called at the end of a request to invoke all {@link RestEndCall} methods on this context.
4735    *
4736    * <p>
4737    * Fires this context's own {@code @RestEndCall} methods against its own resource instance. The host's
4738    * {@code endCall(...)} is invoked from {@link #execute(Object, HttpServletRequest, HttpServletResponse)};
4739    * for mixin-endpoint requests, that path additionally calls {@code endCall} on the mixin sub-context
4740    * after the host's so host hooks fire first, then the mixin's.
4741    *
4742    * @param session The current request.
4743    */
4744   protected void endCall(RestSession session) {
4745      for (var x : endCallInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
4746         try {
4747            x.invoke(session.getBeanStore(), getResource());
4748         } catch (Exception e) {
4749            getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, unwrap(e), () -> String.format("Error occurred invoking finish-call method '%s'.", x.getFullName()));
4750         }
4751      }
4752   }
4753
4754   /**
4755    * Finds the {@link RestOpArg} instances to handle resolving objects on the calls to the specified Java method.
4756    *
4757    * @param m The Java method being called.
4758    * @param beanStore
4759    *    The factory used for creating beans and retrieving injected beans.
4760    *    <br>Created during context bootstrap.
4761    * @return The array of resolvers.
4762    */
4763   protected RestOpArg[] findRestOperationArgs(Method m, BeanStore beanStore) {
4764
4765      var mi = MethodInfo.of(m);
4766      var params = mi.getParameters();
4767      var ra = new RestOpArg[params.size()];
4768
4769      var bs = new BasicBeanStore(beanStore);
4770      var roa = getRestOpArgs();
4771
4772      for (var i = 0; i < params.size(); i++) {
4773         var pi = params.get(i);
4774         bs.addBean(ParameterInfo.class, pi);
4775         for (var c : roa) {
4776            try {
4777               ra[i] = BeanInstantiator.of(RestOpArg.class, bs)
4778                  .type(c)
4779                  .factoryMethodNames("getInstance", "create")
4780                  .factoryAbstainOnNull()
4781                  .run();
4782               if (nn(ra[i]))
4783                  break;
4784            } catch (ExecutableException e) {
4785               throw new InternalServerError(e.unwrap(), "Could not resolve parameter {0} on method {1}.", i, mi.getNameFull());
4786            }
4787         }
4788         if (ra[i] == null)
4789            throw new InternalServerError("Could not resolve parameter {0} on method {1}.", i, mi.getNameFull());
4790      }
4791
4792      return ra;
4793   }
4794
4795   /**
4796    * Returns the time statistics gatherer for the specified method.
4797    *
4798    * @param m The method to get statistics for.
4799    * @return The cached time-stats object.
4800    */
4801   protected MethodExecStats getMethodExecStats(Method m) {
4802      return getMethodExecStore().getStats(m);
4803   }
4804
4805   /**
4806    * Returns the list of methods to invoke after the actual REST method is called.
4807    *
4808    * @return The list of methods to invoke after the actual REST method is called.
4809    */
4810   public MethodList getPostCallMethods() { return postCallMethods.get(); }
4811
4812   /**
4813    * Returns the list of methods to invoke before the actual REST method is called.
4814    *
4815    * @return The list of methods to invoke before the actual REST method is called.
4816    */
4817   public MethodList getPreCallMethods() { return preCallMethods.get(); }
4818
4819   /**
4820    * Returns the list of methods to invoke during servlet destruction.
4821    *
4822    * @return The destroy method list, never {@code null}.
4823    */
4824   public MethodList getDestroyMethods() { return destroyInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4825
4826   /**
4827    * Returns the list of methods to invoke at the end of each HTTP request.
4828    *
4829    * @return The end-call method list, never {@code null}.
4830    */
4831   public MethodList getEndCallMethods() { return endCallInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4832
4833   /**
4834    * Returns the list of methods to invoke during servlet post-initialization (child-last phase).
4835    *
4836    * @return The post-init method list, never {@code null}.
4837    */
4838   public MethodList getPostInitMethods() { return postInitInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4839
4840   /**
4841    * Returns the list of methods to invoke during servlet post-initialization (child-first phase).
4842    *
4843    * @return The post-init-child-first method list, never {@code null}.
4844    */
4845   public MethodList getPostInitChildFirstMethods() { return postInitChildFirstInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4846
4847   /**
4848    * Returns the list of methods to invoke at the start of each HTTP request.
4849    *
4850    * @return The start-call method list, never {@code null}.
4851    */
4852   public MethodList getStartCallMethods() { return startCallInvokerPair.get().methods; }
4853
4854   /**
4855    * Method for handling response errors.
4856    *
4857    * <p>
4858    * Subclasses can override this method to provide their own custom error response handling.
4859    *
4860    * @param session The rest call.
4861    * @param e The exception that occurred.
4862    * @throws IOException Can be thrown if a problem occurred trying to write to the output stream.
4863    */
4864   protected synchronized void handleError(RestSession session, Throwable e) throws IOException {
4865
4866      session.exception(e);
4867
4868      if (session.isDebug())
4869         getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, e, () -> "Error occurred during REST call.");
4870
4871      int code = 500;
4872
4873      var ci = ClassInfo.of(e);
4874      var r = ci.getAnnotations(StatusCode.class).findFirst().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).orElse(null);
4875      if (nn(r) && r.value().length > 0)
4876         code = r.value()[0];
4877
4878      var e2 = (e instanceof BasicHttpException e22 ? e22 : new BasicHttpException(code, null, e));
4879
4880      var req = session.getRequest();
4881      var res = session.getResponse();
4882
4883      Throwable t = e2.getRootCause();
4884      if (nn(t)) {
4885         Thrown t2 = Thrown.of(t);
4886         res.setHeader(t2.getName(), t2.getValue());
4887      }
4888
4889      try {
4890         var statusCode = e2.getStatusLine().getStatusCode();
4891
4892         if (e2 instanceof ValidationException ve && writeValidationErrorBody(res, ve, statusCode, isProblemDetails()))
4893            return;
4894
4895         if (isProblemDetails() && writeProblemDetailsBody(res, e2, statusCode))
4896            return;
4897
4898         res.setContentType("text/plain");
4899         res.setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
4900         res.setStatus(statusCode);
4901
4902         PrintWriter w = getResponseWriter(res);
4903
4904         try (PrintWriter w2 = w) {
4905            var httpMessage = RestUtils.getHttpResponseText(statusCode);
4906            if (nn(httpMessage))
4907               w2.append("HTTP ").append(String.valueOf(statusCode)).append(": ").append(httpMessage).append("\n\n");
4908            if (isRenderResponseStackTraces())
4909               e.printStackTrace(w2);
4910            else
4911               w2.append(e2.getFullStackMessage(true));
4912         }
4913
4914      } catch (Exception e1) {
4915         req.setAttribute("Exception", e1);
4916      }
4917   }
4918
4919   /**
4920    * Writes a {@code BasicHttpException} as an {@code application/problem+json} body. Q5(A): the {@code Accept}
4921    * header is intentionally ignored on the error path so opted-in resources always emit Problem JSON for thrown
4922    * exceptions.
4923    *
4924    * @return {@code true} if the body was written (response committed). {@code false} on serialization failure so
4925    *    the caller can fall back to the legacy {@code text/plain} writer.
4926    */
4927   private static boolean writeProblemDetailsBody(HttpServletResponse res, BasicHttpException e, int statusCode) {
4928      try {
4929         var problem = ProblemAdapters.fromException(e);
4930         res.setStatus(statusCode);
4931         res.setContentType(ContentType.APPLICATION_PROBLEM_JSON.getValue());
4932         res.setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
4933         var os = res.getOutputStream();
4934         JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(problem, os);
4935         os.flush();
4936         return true;
4937      } catch (Exception ex) {
4938         return false;
4939      }
4940   }
4941
4942   /**
4943    * Writes a {@link ValidationException} as a structured JSON body carrying the per-field
4944    * {@link ValidationViolation} list, so client UIs can render field-level errors.
4945    *
4946    * <p>
4947    * Two shapes depending on the resource's {@code @Rest(problemDetails)} opt-in:
4948    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
4949    *    <li><b>Problem-details ON</b> &mdash; emits {@code application/problem+json} with the standard
4950    *       RFC 7807 {@code status}/{@code title}/{@code detail} members plus an {@code errors[]} extension
4951    *       array populated from {@link ValidationException#getViolations()}. Bypasses the generic
4952    *       {@link ProblemAdapters#fromException(BasicHttpException)} path because that adapter is
4953    *       intentionally narrow (no extensions) &mdash; validation needs its own per-field payload.
4954    *    <li><b>Problem-details OFF (default)</b> &mdash; emits {@code application/json} with the simple
4955    *       {@code { "status":400, "errors":[ ... ] }} envelope. Keeps the response usable from clients that
4956    *       can't parse {@code application/problem+json} without making validation responses look like a
4957    *       text/plain stack trace.
4958    * </ul>
4959    *
4960    * @return {@code true} if the body was written (response committed). {@code false} on serialization
4961    *    failure so the caller can fall back to the legacy {@code text/plain} writer.
4962    */
4963   private static boolean writeValidationErrorBody(HttpServletResponse res, ValidationException ve, int statusCode, boolean problemDetails) {
4964      try {
4965         res.setStatus(statusCode);
4966         res.setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
4967         var os = res.getOutputStream();
4968         if (problemDetails) {
4969            var problem = new Problem()
4970               .setStatus(statusCode)
4971               .setTitle(ve.getStatusLine().getReasonPhrase())
4972               .setDetail(ve.getMessage())
4973               .set("errors", ve.getViolations());
4974            res.setContentType(ContentType.APPLICATION_PROBLEM_JSON.getValue());
4975            JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(problem, os);
4976         } else {
4977            var envelope = new LinkedHashMap<String,Object>();
4978            envelope.put("status", statusCode);
4979            envelope.put("errors", ve.getViolations());
4980            res.setContentType("application/json");
4981            JsonSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(envelope, os);
4982         }
4983         os.flush();
4984         return true;
4985      } catch (Exception ex) {
4986         return false;
4987      }
4988   }
4989
4990   /**
4991    * Handle the case where a matching method was not found.
4992    *
4993    * <p>
4994    * Subclasses can override this method to provide a 2nd-chance for specifying a response.
4995    * The default implementation will simply throw an exception with an appropriate message.
4996    *
4997    * @param session The HTTP call.
4998    * @throws Exception Any exception can be thrown.
4999    */
5000   void invokeRestInitMethod(MethodInfo m, Supplier<?> resource, BeanStore beanStore) throws ServletException {
5001      try {
5002         m.inject(beanStore, resource.get());
5003      } catch (Exception e) {
5004         throw servletException(e, "Exception thrown from @RestInit method {0}.{1}.", cns(m.getDeclaringClass()), m.getSignature());
5005      }
5006   }
5007
5008   private static PrintWriter getResponseWriter(HttpServletResponse res) throws IOException {
5009      try {
5010         return res.getWriter();
5011      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") IllegalStateException x) {
5012         return new PrintWriter(new OutputStreamWriter(res.getOutputStream(), UTF8));
5013      }
5014   }
5015
5016   @SuppressWarnings({
5017      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
5018   })
5019   protected void handleNotFound(RestSession session) throws Exception {
5020      var pathInfo = session.getPathInfo();
5021      var methodUC = session.getMethod();
5022      var rc = session.getStatus();
5023      var onPath = pathInfo == null ? " on no pathInfo" : (" on path '" + pathInfo + "'");
5024      if (rc == SC_NOT_FOUND)
5025         throw new NotFound("Method ''{0}'' not found on resource with matching pattern{1}.", methodUC, onPath);
5026      else if (rc == SC_PRECONDITION_FAILED)
5027         throw new PreconditionFailed("Method ''{0}'' not found on resource{1} with matching matcher.", methodUC, onPath);
5028      else if (rc == SC_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED)
5029         throw new MethodNotAllowed("Method ''{0}'' not found on resource{1}.", methodUC, onPath);
5030      else
5031         throw new ServletException("Invalid method response: " + rc, session.getException());
5032   }
5033
5034   /**
5035    * Called during a request to invoke all {@link RestPostCall} methods.
5036    *
5037    * <p>
5038    * For mixin-endpoint requests, fires the host's {@code @RestPostCall} methods first (via the host's
5039    * {@code localPostCallInvokers}), then the mixin's per-op invokers ({@link RestOpContext#getPostCallMethods()}).
5040    * Host-endpoint requests fire only the host's per-op invokers (the established path; unchanged from pre-10.0.0).
5041    *
5042    * @param session The current request.
5043    * @throws Exception If thrown from call methods.
5044    */
5045   protected void postCall(RestOpSession session) throws Exception {
5046      var opCtx = session.getContext().getContext();
5047      if (opCtx.isMixinContext && opCtx.parentContext != null)
5048         for (var m : opCtx.parentContext.localPostCallInvokers.get())
5049            m.invoke(session);
5050      for (var m : session.getContext().getPostCallMethods())
5051         m.invoke(session);
5052   }
5053
5054   /**
5055    * Called during a request to invoke all {@link RestPreCall} methods.
5056    *
5057    * <p>
5058    * For mixin-endpoint requests, fires the host's {@code @RestPreCall} methods first (via the host's
5059    * {@code localPreCallInvokers}), then the mixin's per-op invokers ({@link RestOpContext#getPreCallMethods()}).
5060    * Host-endpoint requests fire only the host's per-op invokers (the established path; unchanged from pre-10.0.0).
5061    *
5062    * @param session The current request.
5063    * @throws Exception If thrown from call methods.
5064    */
5065   protected void preCall(RestOpSession session) throws Exception {
5066      var opCtx = session.getContext().getContext();
5067      if (opCtx.isMixinContext && opCtx.parentContext != null)
5068         for (var m : opCtx.parentContext.localPreCallInvokers.get())
5069            m.invoke(session);
5070      for (var m : session.getContext().getPreCallMethods())
5071         m.invoke(session);
5072   }
5073
5074   /**
5075    * The main method for serializing POJOs passed in through the {@link RestResponse#setContent(Object)} method or
5076    * returned by the Java method.
5077    *
5078    * <p>
5079    * Subclasses may override this method if they wish to modify the way the output is rendered or support other output
5080    * formats.
5081    *
5082    * <p>
5083    * The default implementation simply iterates through the response handlers on this resource
5084    * looking for the first one whose {@link ResponseProcessor#process(RestOpSession)} method returns
5085    * <jk>true</jk>.
5086    *
5087    * @param opSession The HTTP call.
5088    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
5089    * @throws BasicHttpException Non-200 response.
5090    * @throws NotImplemented No registered response processors could handle the call.
5091    */
5092   @SuppressWarnings({
5093      "java:S127" // Loop counter i resets to -1 on RESTART
5094   })
5095   public void processResponse(RestOpSession opSession) throws IOException, BasicHttpException, NotImplemented {
5096
5097      // Loop until we find the correct processor for the POJO.
5098      int loops = 5;
5099      var rp = getResponseProcessors();
5100      for (var i = 0; i < rp.length; i++) {
5101         var j = rp[i].process(opSession);
5102         if (j == FINISHED)
5103            return;
5104         if (j == RESTART) {
5105            if (loops-- < 0)
5106               throw new InternalServerError("Too many processing loops.");
5107            i = -1;  // Start over.
5108         }
5109      }
5110
5111      var output = opSession.getResponse().getContent().orElse(null);
5112      throw new NotImplemented("No response processors found to process output of type ''{0}''", cn(output));
5113   }
5114
5115   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
5116   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
5117      return super.properties()
5118         .a(PROP_allowContentParam, isAllowContentParam())
5119         .a(PROPERTY_allowedHeaderParams, getAllowedHeaderParams())
5120         .a(PROPERTY_allowedMethodHeaders, getAllowedMethodHeaders())
5121         .a(PROPERTY_allowedMethodParams, getAllowedMethodParams())
5122         .a(PROP_beanStore, beanStore)
5123         .a(PROPERTY_clientVersionHeader, getClientVersionHeader())
5124         .a(PROP_consumes, getConsumes())
5125         .a(PROP_defaultRequestHeaders, getDefaultRequestHeaders())
5126         .a(PROP_defaultResponseHeaders, getDefaultResponseHeaders())
5127         .a(PROP_partParser, getPartParser())
5128         .a(PROP_partSerializer, getPartSerializer())
5129         .a(PROP_produces, getProduces())
5130         .a(PROPERTY_renderResponseStackTraces, isRenderResponseStackTraces())
5131         .a(PROP_responseProcessors, getResponseProcessors())
5132         .a(PROP_restOpArgs, getRestOpArgs())
5133         .a(PROP_staticFiles, getStaticFiles())
5134         .a(PROP_swaggerProvider, getSwaggerProvider())
5135         .a(PROP_openApiProvider, getOpenApiProvider())
5136         .a(PROPERTY_uriAuthority, getUriAuthority())
5137         .a(PROPERTY_uriContext, getUriContext())
5138         .a(PROPERTY_uriRelativity, getUriRelativity())
5139         .a(PROPERTY_uriResolution, getUriResolution());
5140   }
5141
5142   /**
5143    * Called at the start of a request to invoke all {@link RestStartCall} methods on this context.
5144    *
5145    * <p>
5146    * Fires this context's own {@code @RestStartCall} methods against its own resource instance. The host
5147    * version is invoked from {@link #execute(Object, HttpServletRequest, HttpServletResponse)} before the
5148    * operation is resolved; once the operation is known to belong to a mixin sub-context,
5149    * {@link RestSession#run()} additionally calls {@code startCall} on the mixin context so host hooks fire
5150    * first, then the mixin's.
5151    *
5152    * @param session The current request.
5153    * @throws BasicHttpException If thrown from call methods.
5154    */
5155   protected void startCall(RestSession session) throws BasicHttpException {
5156      for (var x : startCallInvokerPair.get().invokers) {
5157         try {
5158            x.invoke(session.getBeanStore(), getResource());
5159         } catch (IllegalAccessException | IllegalArgumentException e) {
5160            throw new InternalServerError(e, "Error occurred invoking start-call method ''{0}''.", x.getFullName());
5161         } catch (InvocationTargetException e) {
5162            var t = e.getTargetException();
5163            if (t instanceof BasicHttpException t2)
5164               throw t2;
5165            throw new InternalServerError(t);
5166         }
5167      }
5168   }
5169}
